title: 60 Avoca Street, South Yarra, 3141
heading: A Generational Domain Hill Opportunity
description_plain:
Expressions of Interest Close 11th May at 3pm.
Inspect By Private Appointment.

Among Domain Hill’s most tightly held addresses, this landmark c1850 Victorian estate occupies approximately 760sqm, with grand proportions and a prized northern orientation that brings natural light deep into the home’s principal rooms. Its long lineage, shaped by ornate 1870s revival and later refinement by notable architects including the Freeman Brothers, Neil Clerehan and, most recently, Allan Powell, is further distinguished by its feature in Home Beautiful magazine in 1952, and it is now offered for sale for the first time in almost four decades.

Beyond a formal forecourt with tessellated tiles and original iron fencing, the interior is replete with soaring ceilings, intricate cornicing, and fireplaces now enhanced for modern use, leading through a collection of lavishly sized reception rooms to a north-facing kitchen appointed with Italian marble and Smeg appliances. Living areas continue to a library-lined sitting room with an integrated bar, and into a sunroom at the rear, which opens via a series of French doors to a deep terrace shaded by ornamental grape and overlooking richly planted gardens.

A substantial, sun-drenched main bedroom sits beneath breathtaking vaulted ceilings upstairs, enriched by an ornate marble fireplace, a dressing room, and a book-matched marble ensuite. Three additional bedrooms, one with its own ensuite, plus a third bathroom follow to the rear, where a wide Queenslander-style verandah takes in the greenery below. Well-appointed with hydronic heating, security, garage, and a powder room, this magnificent residence presents a rare opportunity to carry forward a long-held legacy or to refine for contemporary living, moments from Toorak Road dining, the Royal Botanic Gardens Victoria, and leading schools including Melbourne Grammar School, Melbourne Girls’ Grammar School, Christ Church Grammar School, Melbourne High School, Mac.Robertson Girls' High School and St Kevin’s College, with South Yarra Station close by for direct CBD access and also trams along Toorak Road.

title: 2/51 Mathoura Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Elegant, North-Facing Garden Residence
description_plain:
Expressions of Interest

Exclusively positioned between Toorak and Hawksburn Villages, this elegant ground-floor residence with an internal size of 297 sqm, one of only five, delivers refined lock-up and leave living through its grand proportions, curated finishes, and beautifully private garden surrounds, with every principal room opening to established north-facing outdoor spaces shaped by the architectural vision of Nicholas Day and landscaped by Paul Bangay.

A wide gallery hall and foyer introduce richly toned chevron oak floors, ornate cornicing, and impressive ceiling heights, creating an immediate impact that continues through the expansive living domains. A gas fire adds warmth alongside custom storage and a sitting area, adjoining a separate dining room and a granite-appointed kitchen with a full suite of Miele appliances, including an integrated espresso machine. French doors from each space draw the living zones outward to the striking entertainer’s terrace, encouraging a natural indoor-outdoor flow for entertaining set beside a tranquil water feature and elevated, irrigated gardens.
Generous accommodation includes a lavishly sized main bedroom with terrace access, a dressing room lined with mirrored robes, and a deluxe ensuite with a freestanding bath. Two additional bedrooms also open to private courtyards and feature built-in robes and equally stunning en-suites, whilst an executive study with custom joinery adds further flexibility. With all principal rooms opening to the outdoors.

Comprehensively appointed with zoned ducted heating and cooling, alarm, security intercom, full laundry, powder room, abundant storage and secure basement garaging for four vehicles with lift access, it sits just moments from Toorak Road trams, Hawksburn station, Brookville Park and Melbourne’s leading schools.

title: 6/67 High Street, Prahran, 3181
heading: Lofty, Old School, Architectural Conversion
description_plain:
The stunning architectural conversion of what was an iconic 1880s Prahran State School has created this lofty, two bedroom split-level townhouse-like apartment with leafy outlooks and a back-of-the-block serenity that is utterly surreal for such a vibrant urban location. Beyond its exquisite period façade, the home is securely accessed via intercom entry and a bluestone-paved foyer before unfolding under soaring ceilings to create a light-filled sense of period grandeur in one of Melbourne’s most coveted lifestyle hubs just a short stroll from Chapel Street.

Instantly captivating under a soaring double-height entrance void, the apartment steps down to vast open-plan dining / living that is anchored by a kitchen with Blanco cooking and seamlessly connected to a partially bluestone-paved courtyard that wraps around to catch northern sun but to also offer the convenience of directed gated access to a secure carpark where carport parking awaits. Freshly painted throughout, the apartment steps upstairs to a pair of freshly carpeted bedrooms with built-in robes, leafy treetop outlooks and easy access to a bright bathroom, while further highlights include a downstairs powder room, concealed laundry and split system heating / air conditioning.

Well removed from the street and enjoying back-of-the-block sanctuary, the apartment allows you to spend your mornings in search of your favourite local barista or the best fresh produce from Prahran Market and your weekends amidst the vintage and vinyl of Greville Street, whilst Hawker Hall, The Emerson and Tyranny are perfect for some weekend mischief, plus you have Albert Park Lake nearby and all modes of transport nearby – including Prahran Railway Station just a few minutes away and the Number 6 tram just across the road for an effortless city commute in next to no time.

title: 606 Toorak Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Landmark Toorak Opportunity on 2,230sqm approx.
description_plain:
On an expansive allotment of 2,230sqm* in a AAA Toorak setting, this vacant, north-facing landholding presents a powerful opportunity to create a landmark residence surrounded by some of Melbourne’s most admired homes, with proportions that allow a truly substantial home to be realised.

Cleared and ready for construction, this impressive site with a commanding 36m* frontage to Toorak Road offers a rare blank canvas for a bold architectural vision shaped by orientation and privacy (STCA).

Moments from Kooyong Village, Toorak Village and the Kooyong Lawn Tennis Club, with trams, Kooyong Station and Monash Freeway access close by, and within reach of St Kevin’s College, Scotch College, St Catherine’s School and Lauriston Girls’ School, opportunities of this scale in Toorak are rarely offered.

*approximate land size

title: 6/50 Grange Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Bespoke Renovation with a Mid-Century Soul
description_plain:
A genuinely bespoke mid-century classic that boldly celebrates light and proportion, this flawless two bedroom apartment has been superbly renovated, at the very rear of a proudly maintained collection of just eight, behind the iconic trees of one of the area’s most coveted roads. Perfectly nestled between the charms of Toorak Village and the banks of the Yarra, this is a grandly elevated ground floor residence with the additional prize of secure carport parking. 

Timelessly elegant with granite benchtops and plenty of cabinetry, the kitchen / meals also reveals a suite of European appliances that include Smeg cooking and a new Miele pyrolytic air-fryer oven, all of which overlooks semi-attached dining and a large lounge where the northern light is glorious. Securely accessed via intercom entry, the apartment also features particularly spacious bedrooms with mirrored built-in robes – and a sun drenched terrace off the primary suite – as well as freshly sanded original hardwood floors, new reverse cycle heating / air conditioning, new curtains and a proudly renovated showpiece bathroom and laundry - crafted by the award-winning GIA Renovations - where full Italian tiling is complemented by Blue Patagonia Marble surfaces, a fluted shower screen, circular bathtub and sub-vanity lighting as well as a handblown glass mirror light to completely wow the senses.

Poised to enjoy all the trappings of a Toorak lifestyle within easy walking distance of the refined café culture, thriving bars, restaurants and boutique shopping of the village, where city-bound trams can take you to Chapel Street in no time, whilst you also have all the endless parkland and bike paths of the Yarra River just at the other end of the road.

title: Units 1-7/36-38 Albion Street, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Endless Options in Enviable Location
description_plain:
Nestled within a prized location just a short walk to every South Yarra drawcard, this superb enclave of seven apartments represents a rewarding addition to any investment portfolio or a sensational site to build a new luxury home or boutique development (STCA).

Currently leased, the three one-bedroom and four studio apartments are in ideal condition to continue as a lucrative income generator in a highly coveted inner urban location close to public transport, Prahran Market, Chapel Street, Commercial Road and Toorak Road shops and eateries and Fawkner Park. Passing rental $136,000 per annum approx.

Alternatively, in a quiet, peaceful pocket, the superb site provides the perfect opportunity to make an architectural statement with a new home or boutique development designed to your own requirements and tastes.

In a location always in demand, this is an increasingly rare opportunity. 
Land size: 377sqm approx.

title: 302/511 Dandenong Road, Armadale, 3143
heading: Armadale Luxury with North Terrace and City Views
description_plain:
Expressions of Interest Close Thur 2 April 5.00pm

Quietly positioned at the rear of the elegant C. Kairouz Architects-designed Arcadian building, this outstanding third-floor residence delivers a rare combination of boutique luxury, privacy and lifestyle appeal, elevated by a magnificent north-facing terrace. Adjoining no neighbouring apartments, and designed with a considered focus on sophistication, security and liveability, this home delivers exceptional modern family living in one of Armadale’s most connected addresses.

Beyond the secure entry, impeccably curated interiors immediately set the tone. Dark hardwood floors and refined finishes create a sense of understated luxury throughout. The accommodation is beautifully zoned, beginning with a generous main bedroom featuring custom robes and a chic ensuite appointed with porcelain accents and a walk-in rain shower. Two further bedrooms, each with built-in robes, are served by an equally impressive central bathroom, where porcelain detailing, a designer bathtub, dual vanities and a rain shower continue the home’s polished aesthetic.

At the rear, the apartment opens to a stunning open-plan domain showcasing impressive scale and design. The gourmet kitchen is both sleek and highly practical, appointed with premium Miele appliances, extensive cabinetry, and an oversized porcelain breakfast bar island. A superb butler’s pantry adds further functionality, offering ample bench and storage space, ideal for effortless everyday living and entertaining.

The adjoining living and dining area is refined and light-filled, with a striking feature wall, custom shelving, integrated wine storage and a gas fireplace. Full-height sliding glass doors with retractable screening connect the interiors to the expansive terrace, capturing sweeping panoramic views across the city skyline and surrounding districts, creating an exceptional alfresco space.

Additional appointments include ducted heating and cooling, two secure basement car spaces, a storage cage, bicycle racks and excellent storage throughout. Positioned along one of Armadale’s most connected boulevards, with trams at the door, Armadale and Malvern stations within easy reach, and High Street, Glenferrie Road, Malvern Central, leading schools, cafes, boutiques and parklands all close by, this rare terrace apartment perfectly captures the low-maintenance luxury and lifestyle prestige of Armadale living.

title: 1/6a Ruabon Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Boutique, North-facing Tranquility
description_plain:
Expressions of Interest Close Tue 14 April 5.00pm

An exclusive hideaway in one of Melbourne’s most sought-after locales, this state-of-the-art, two bedroom, two bathroom townhouse delivers superbly appointed lock-and-leave convenience in a whisper-quiet, traffic-free setting, just a few metres from Hawksburn Village. Fully secure and remarkably private with a sundrenched alfresco flow and a proud entertainers’ spirit, this is a boutique home that forms a perfect synergy with its prestigious surrounds.

Securely introduced by a north-facing entertainers’-sized deck that captures sunshine all day long, the home fills with northern light as it unfolds to reveal generous open-plan living and dining with engineered Oak flooring, all anchored by a Miele-equipped kitchen where stone benches include an inviting island where four-to-five can sit in comfort. Upstairs, the bedrooms offer generous robed accommodation with enough space for desks in each – walk-through robe access from the primary suite to a fully tiled ensuite that is themed to the central bathroom – while added highlights include a laundry, an alarm and a secure carport parking that can serve as undercover alfresco party zone if needed, plus you have reverse cycle heating / air conditioning.

Walk to some of Melbourne’s pre-eminent cafes, bars and boutique shopping at both Hawksburn Village and Toorak Village from this tranquil position near both Toorak and Hawksburn Railway Stations, Orrong Park and plenty of city-bound trams.

title: 7/27 Wallace Avenue, Toorak, 3142
heading: A Sunlit Classic on the Cusp of the Village
description_plain:
Private Sale Close Tue 14 April 5.00pm 

Securely set on the second floor/top floor where natural sunlight and leafy outlooks deliver endless joy, this impressively scaled and tastefully refurbished two bedroom classic sits atop a tree-surrounded mid-century building right on the cusp of Toorak Village. A gleaming white colour palette complements rich timber-toned flooring in this lofty and larger-than-expected apartment with secure parking and a decadent lifestyle of ritzy local cafes, walks through Como Park and the easiest of access to Chapel Street and the CBD.

A gleaming white kitchen/meals with stainless steel appliances offers a genuinely separate dining area to the home’s spacious lounge/dining room, while an east-facing treetop terrace draws glorious morning sun to create the perfect spot for a leisurely breakfast. Each of the bedrooms is carpeted and completed with built-in robes as well as large light-letting windows, plus there is a sparkling bathroom with an independent bathtub and a separate wc, while split system heating/air conditioning offers year-round enjoyment.

Whether it be your first caffeine of the day, some gourmet food choices, a drink to unwind or any of your shopping needs, it can all be found just a few steps away at the Village, plus you have city-bound trams along Toorak Road, Como Park and the Yarra’s trails also within easy reach.

title: 4/157 Peel Street, Windsor, 3181
heading: Sunlit Style, Windsor Energy
description_plain:
Private Sale Close Tue 14 April 5.00pm

Right in the heart of Windsor and just metres from Chapel Street, with a lifestyle offering that few can match, this sizable, sleek and sophisticated single bedroom first-floor apartment is certain to exceed expectations from within a boutique collection of just eight. Remarkably quiet and spilling out to a generous sun-drawing terrace, this is a first-class opportunity for everyone from those looking to make their first step into the market, to those who are refining their portfolio or searching for an energetically situated lock-and-leave.

Smeg cooking is complemented by an integrated Bosch dishwasher and stone benches in a kitchen that will undoubtedly unload plenty of local takeaway, but will also give you the confidence to start your own gourmet cooking journey, while the terrace is generously scaled, bathed in morning sun and perfect for your first coffee of the day. Conveniently, the bathroom plays host to a large walk-in shower and a concealed laundry, plus there are mirrored robes in the bedroom, engineered Oak flooring to complement exposed concrete ceiling work and give a hint of industrial style, plus there is intercom entry and split system heating/air conditioning. 

Oozing urban attitude from this highly energetic and coveted pocket of Windsor, the whole world of Chapel Street is at your doorstep with an endless selection of cafes, restaurants, bars and nightlife venues, plenty of trams, city-bound trains from Windsor Railway Station and great open expanses nearby at both Alma Park and Albert Park Lake.

title: 63 Davis Avenue, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Style, Space and a Sensational Lifestyle
description_plain: Defined by an impressive sense of space, this sensational three-level contemporary residence designed by award-winning architect Peter Maddison combines sophisticated styling with an unbeatable lifestyle on the edge of Toorak Road’s renowned restaurant and retail precinct. Beneath soaring ceilings and bathed in northern light, the expansive sitting room with a feature stone fireplace and dining area with a cocktail bar opens to a private north facing deck. Oak floors flow through the gourmet kitchen appointed with marble benches, Miele appliances and integrated fridge/freezer, a sun-filled dining nook with window seats and the generous living room. Glass sliders connect the living area with a large northwest facing decked courtyard with access to a rear right of way. A floating steel and timber staircase leads up to the main bedroom with designer ensuite, walk in robe and an undercover balcony, two additional double bedrooms with robes and a stylish bathroom. A large basement garage has capacity for two cars and storage. In a prized tree-lined avenue walking distance to a host of cafes, restaurants and boutiques, South Yarra station, trams, Como Cinemas, Prahran Market, excellent schools and Fawkner Park, it includes an alarm, panel heating, RC/air-conditioners, powder-room, fourth toilet, laundry and auto gate. Land size: 546sqm approx.

title: 4/757 Malvern Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Boutique Elegance with a Blue Chip Lifestyle
description_plain:
Peacefully set at the rear of just four, this stunning boutique residence’s generosity of space, refined style and beautiful garden setting provides secure, serene lock up and leave luxury at its finest.
Reflecting timeless elegance, the inviting interior dimensions featuring oak floors, high ceilings and plantation shutters are awash with northern light. The wide entrance hall flows through to an expansive living room with a gas fireplace and leafy courtyards at either end. Sliders open to the private north-facing paved courtyard ideal for al fresco dining beneath the canopy of an established tree. Also enjoying a courtyard outlook the generous dining room is served by a sleek gourmet kitchen appointed with stone benches and stainless-steel appliances. A spacious downstairs bedroom looking out to a courtyard is replete with walk in robe and a marble ensuite. Upstairs, two additional double bedrooms are accompanied by a bright bathroom with bath and landing study area.
Enviably situated just a short walk to Beatty Ave, Toorak station, High St shops, Hawksburn and Toorak Villages, elite schools, trams and Orrong Park it includes an alarm, security intercom, zoned heating/cooling, powder-room, laundry, internally accessed double garage with storeroom and auto gates.

title: 23 Egerton Road, Armadale, 3143
heading: Consummate Contemporary Luxury and Refinement
description_plain:
Showcasing palatial proportions, lavish finishes and captivating landscaped outdoor surrounds, this stunning architect-designed four-bedroom, three-bathroom contemporary residence delivers an unrivalled executive lifestyle moments to every Armadale drawcard.
The brilliant design allows all living spaces to embrace the glorious peaceful garden surrounds through floor-to-ceiling glass. American oak floors flow through an extra-broad central hallway to an expansive sitting room opening to the north and east facing gardens where a vine-covered pergola and large decked area overlook the tranquil fishpond with waterfall. The spectacular living room with a gas fire and equally spacious dining area also open through stacking glass sliders to a Vergola-covered al fresco dining terrace with built-in BBQ and heater. Impeccably catering to any culinary challenge, the sleek gourmet kitchen is appointed with Miele appliances and stone benches. Sky-lit stairs lead up to the generous main bedroom with walk-in robe and designer ensuite, two additional robed bedrooms, a home office or fourth bedroom and a stylish bathroom with freestanding bath.
In a prized location only minutes to High St and Glenferrie Rd shops and restaurants, Malvern Central, Armadale and Malvern stations, trams and excellent schools, it is comprehensively appointed with an alarm, video intercom, zoned heating/cooling, ducted vacuum, plantation shutters, double glazing, third bathroom, laundry, ample storage, irrigation water tank and internally accessed double garage.

title: 328/111 Punt Road, Windsor, 3181
heading: Renovated chic with endless lifestyle appeal
description_plain:
Expressions of Interest Close Thur 9 April 6.00pm

Set well-back from any thoroughfares to be a quiet retreat from the nearby bustle, this revitalised prize offers lock-and-leave ease, endless lifestyle appeal, and a calibre of luxury rare at such an accessible price point.
 
Sure to surpass any and all expectations, flowing interiors are just as attractive as they are practical, with an oversized bedroom served by excellent robe storage and a chic ensuite. Augmented with a built-in bar, a sun-soaked living/dining space spills onto a northwest-facing balcony brilliant for a drink watching the sunset, with any occasion comfortably catered for by a gourmet kitchen’s stone benches and top-of-the-range Bosch downdraft cooktop, oven, integrated fridge/freezer, and dishwasher. An elegant second bathroom is ideal for visitors, while a long list of further highlights includes herringbone oak parquetry, reverse-cycle heating/cooling, sheer curtains, stone vanity tops, brushed-brass tapware, an intercom/security entry, European laundry, and secure car space.
 
The perfect professional’s abode, downsizer’s domain, or part-time base for regular travellers and those who balance city and county living, it’s never more than a short stroll to tranquil Albert Park Lake, lively Fitzroy Street, Prahran Market and Station, and St Kilda Road trams, along with the cuisine, retail, and nightlife of a true Melbourne icon in Chapel Street.

title: 109 Caroline Street, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Blue Chip Entire-Block Opportunity on 651sqm approx.
description_plain:
Commanding a premier city-fringe position beside the Yarra River and only moments from the Royal Botanic Gardens, this solid brick Art Deco block of four substantial residences presents a compelling investment or redevelopment opportunity* on a prized 651sqm approx. corner allotment. Generous proportions, secure garaging and established tenancies provide immediate income, while the outstanding location on the edge of The Domain places Melbourne’s sporting precinct, leading independent schools and the CBD all within easy reach.
Occupying the upper level, the dual-level, privately accessed penthouse residence is the largest of the four and delivers impressive three-bedroom, two-bath accommodation with sweeping outlooks and expansive terraces designed to capture light and privacy, complemented by a private double garage with internal entry. Units one and two provide light-filled two-bedroom, one-bathroom layouts with private courtyard gardens, balconies, open living and dining zones and secure garaging, accessed via a shared secure entry.

With the Yarra River trails, transport, The Domain and elite schools all within walking distance, this landmark property presents an exceptional opportunity as a high-performing investment or future development site in one of Melbourne’s most tightly-held pockets. *Subject to council approval.

Floorplans available upon request.

title: 2/82 Mathoura Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Peerless Luxury and Lifestyle
description_plain:
Exclusively situated in a prized address between Toorak and Hawksburn Villages, this magnificent ground level boutique residence, one of only five, brilliantly epitomises luxurious lock up and leave living with its palatial proportions, opulent finishes and tranquil garden surrounds.

Reflecting the signature design and quality of a Demaine Partnership development, rich European oak floors and 3.3m ceilings are displayed through the wide reception hall, grand sitting room with a gas log fire and the equally expansive dining room. A series of double-glazed doors open the living spaces to the entirely private north and east facing garden terraces with a water feature and paved area for al fresco dining. At the heart of the home, a superb gourmet kitchen appointed with granite benches and a full complement of Miele appliances caters to every culinary requirement. The lavishly proportioned main bedroom with a designer marble ensuite, bath and dressing room and an adjacent fitted executive study are set apart from two additional double bedrooms with built in robes and stylish ensuites, all opening to the garden.

Comprehensively appointed with an alarm, video intercom, RC/air-conditioning throughout, powder-room, laundry, 2 basement storerooms and 3 basement car-spaces, it’s just a short walk to Toorak Rd trams, Hawksburn station, Brookville Park and excellent schools.

title: G03/5 Brookville Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Brand New North Facing Garden Residence
description_plain:
The Brookville sets a new benchmark for refined apartment living, where exceptional craftsmanship and timeless design come together in one of Toorak’s most prestigious addresses. Designed by the acclaimed team at Carr Design, this landmark residence reflects the elegance and sophistication long associated with Melbourne’s most coveted suburb.

This brand-new ground-floor garden residence offers an impressive sense of space and luxury from the moment you step inside. The main bedroom is a private retreat, complete with a generous walk-in robe and a beautifully finished ensuite featuring a freestanding bath, rainfall shower and luxurious underfloor heating. Two further bedrooms with built-in robes are serviced by a stylish central bathroom, while a separate powder room adds convenience for guests.

The heart of the home is a striking contemporary kitchen designed for both everyday living and effortless entertaining. Full-height marble splashbacks, a statement timber waterfall island and premium V-ZUG appliances - including a five-burner gas cooktop, warming drawer and integrated dishwasher -  are complemented by a Sub-Zero integrated fridge/freezer, delivering both performance and style.

Bathed in natural light, the expansive living and dining area opens seamlessly to a generous north-facing courtyard, creating a private outdoor sanctuary perfect for relaxed entertaining or quiet enjoyment.

Additional features include secure basement parking for two cars, private lift access and exclusive entry points, ensuring the highest levels of comfort, security and convenience.

Inspired by the principles of Australian Modernism, The Brookville honours Toorak’s architectural heritage while introducing a sophisticated new standard of living. Ideally positioned within walking distance of Toorak and Hawksburn Villages, Brookville Gardens and excellent transport connections, this residence places the very best of the suburb at your doorstep.

Discover The Brookville - a rare opportunity to experience contemporary luxury in one of Melbourne’s most sought-after locations.

title: 207/105 High Street, Prahran, 3181
heading: ‘Marque’: Architecturally Curated Excellence
description_plain:
Expressions Of Interest Close Tue 31 March 6.00pm

Proudly displaying all the understated elegance and sophistication of design we expect of premium Fender Katsalidis architecturally curated build, this evocative two bedroom, two bathroom ‘Marque’ apartment is fully secure and wrapped in double glazing, allowing you to enjoy a surreal sense of sanctuary within easy reach of Chapel Street. Distinguished by quality and positioned for lifestyle, this is a superior, second floor urban offering crafted to exceed expectations.

Organised around a broad waterfall-edged island bench of marble that can comfortably seat five, the open-plan dining / living room is anchored by a gourmet Miele-equipped kitchen with plenty of soft close cabinetry to retain the integrity of its sleek clean lines. Huge double-glazed sliding doors extend the living space outdoors to an oversized undercover terrace where the views across southern Melbourne are uninterrupted, while both the primary bedroom’s ensuite and the main bathroom are especially vogue and each of the bedrooms offer built-in robes as well as access to either of the home’s two terraces. Fully secure with video entry, the apartment also features secure non-stacker basement parking with storage conveniently adjacent to a fob-activated elevator, plus there is CCTV surveillance, a concealed laundry and two split systems for winter warmth and summer relief.

Start your day with a coffee from Tone or any of the other countless great local options, plus you have fine dining all around you, great bars and teeming nightlife on Chapel and Greville Streets, as well as city-bound trains from Prahran Railway Station, multiple trams and Prahran Market nearby for all your gourmet and fresh produce requirements, while the vast expanses of Albert Park Lake are just down the road.

title: 18a Ferncroft Avenue, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Parkside Privacy and Seclusion
description_plain:
Expressions of Interest close Tuesday 31 March 3.00pm

An exclusive and enviable position directly fronting Hedgeley Dene Gardens sets the tone for this impressive freestanding residence, privately nestled within beautifully landscaped gardens and complemented by a sparkling pool and spa. Enjoying a privileged outlook, this immaculate contemporary home delivers a peaceful, park-side sanctuary with an executive lifestyle of exceptional comfort and ease.

Secluded beneath a leafy canopy and designed to maximise natural light and garden views, the superbly constructed two-level layout showcases quality and versatility throughout. Upstairs, a vast and flexible domain functions effortlessly as an oversized bedroom with walk-in robe and ensuite, or alternatively as an additional living retreat, enhanced by an adjoining storeroom or playroom.

The ground floor is perfectly proportioned for both family living and entertaining, with a series of beautifully appointed rooms and alfresco zones centred around a generous family hub. The main bedroom suite and two additional bedrooms each feature walk-in robes and private ensuites, while a fitted home office with integrated desks and storage provides a refined work-from-home environment. A gracious formal sitting room with gas fireplace and French doors to the deck, powder room and well-equipped laundry further elevate everyday functionality.

At the heart of the home, open-plan living and dining areas with fireplace and bifold doors extend seamlessly to a picturesque garden setting, undercover entertaining terrace and heated tiled pool/spa. The gourmet stone kitchen is appointed with Miele and Gaggenau appliances and a butler’s pantry, induction cooktop, steamer oven and two integrated fridge/freezers, ensuring effortless hosting.

Additional highlights include hydronic heating, ducted cooling, video intercom, polished hardwood floors, plantation shutters, LED lighting, rainwater tank and an internally accessed remote double garage.
Set on 600sqm* with direct park frontage, this low-maintenance sanctuary offers exceptional proximity to Darling Village, Gardiner Creek trails, Central Park Village and its tram terminus and fine restaurants,  leading schools including Korowa Anglican Girls' School and Sacre Coeur, as well as Monash University, Chadstone Shopping Centre and the Monash Freeway, delivering an enviable lifestyle in one of the area’s most tightly held park-side positions.

*approximate land size

title: 5/5 Gooch Street, Prahran, 3181
heading: Parkside Lifestyle Dream in Prahran
description_plain:
Expressions of Interest Close Tue 31 March 5.00pm

Perfectly positioned with Lumley Gardens at your doorstep, High Street in your orbit and Chapel Street on speed dial, this stylishly rejuvenated single bedroom apartment delivers all the fruits of an addictive Prahran lifestyle without even a hint of compromise. Fully secure and with undercover parking to seal the deal, this ready-to-be-enjoyed apartment is a brilliant option in the right postcode for lifestyle-motivated first home buyers, with all the right credentials for savvy investors and the easy city access so coveted by those seeking a lock-and-leave base.

Stone benchtops, stainless steel appliances and plantation shutters form the basis of a confident kitchen that is crafted for genuine cooking, while a separate lounge / dining features plush carpets and electric wall heating. The bathroom / laundry is particularly stylish, fully tiled and separate to the wc, plus there is intercom entry and, of course, undercover parking.

Close enough to walk to all the dining options, bars and nightlife of High Street and Chapel Street, but far enough away to enjoy some genuine parkside sanctuary, this is a terrific location just moments from Prahran Market and Prahran Pool, well serviced by multiple trams, buses and trains from Hawksburn Railway Station.

title: 4/2a Struan Street, Toorak, 3142
heading: Gracious Proportions in a Boutique Block
description_plain:
Expressions of Interest Close 2nd April at 3pm.

Beautifully presented and exclusively positioned within an immaculate boutique block of only five, this first-floor lift-connected residence delivers generous proportions, abundant northern light and an easy indoor-outdoor lifestyle in one of Toorak’s most picturesque tree-lined streets.

Secure entry leads to a wide hallway that introduces elegant living and dining areas designed for both relaxed day-to-day living and entertaining. The well-appointed kitchen with European appliances and granite benchtops connects to informal meals, while expansive glazing draws natural light deep into the interiors and opens to a large north-facing terrace, creating a private outdoor setting for year-round use. Accommodation is substantial, with a large main bedroom featuring a walk-in robe and spa ensuite, complemented by two further bedrooms, each with its own walk-in robe and ensuite.

within an easy walk to Toorak Village’s cafés, restaurants and boutiques, with Como Park, transport and leading schools close by, additional features include ducted heating and cooling, intercom, laundry, powder room, abundant storage, side-by-side basement parking for two and a secure storage room, delivering a low-maintenance lifestyle in a tightly-held position.

title: 72 Bendigo Street, Prahran, 3181
heading: Picture Perfect Victorian Cottage
description_plain:
Positioned at the preferred northern end of an iconic row of Victorian cottages, within a surrounding period streetscape, this classic single fronted cottage is as pretty as a picture with its iron lacework verandah. Presenting an outstanding opportunity to capitalise on a chic and vibrant lifestyle close to Chapel Street, Hawksburn Village or the many South Yarra lifestyle amenities. 

A previously renovated interior has plenty of northern light and provides an opportunity to move in and enjoy the lifestyle. The interior features high ceilings with dark stained floors flowing through an arched hallway to two double bedrooms both with built-in robes and a stylish bathroom and laundry combined. A spacious full width dining room sits alongside a sleek kitchen equipped with Emelia and Bellini stainless steel appliances plus a light filled living room opening through French doors to a leafy, paved low maintenance courtyard and deck – simply perfect for relaxed indoor/outdoor entertaining. Other features include R/C air conditioners (main bedroom and living area) and parking permits are available.

Enviably located in an ultra-convenient location with the myriad of fashion outlets, cafes, restaurants and entertainment amenities offered by Chapel Street, Malvern and Toorak Roads – as well as quick access to the CBD via the transport options including bus along Williams Road, Hawksburn Station, High Street or the Malvern Road trams. Enjoy the local Flying Duck Hotel plus walking distance to Prahran Market and numerous parklands in the surrounding area. All offering a sought-after opportunity for those seeking a convenient, low maintenance lifestyle in an exclusive locale with walk-to dining and recreation options.

title: 1/53 Cromwell Road, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Contemporary Luxury, Lifestyle Locale
description_plain:
A masterfully reimagined residence of rare scale and refinement, this stunning contemporary home offers a sophisticated sanctuary in one of Melbourne’s most coveted lifestyle precincts. Positioned just moments from the vibrant energy of Chapel Street and the curated elegance of Toorak Road, the property delivers an unrivalled level of connectivity to boutique shopping, celebrated dining, and the iconic Prahran Market. For those seeking a balance of wellness and convenience, the tranquil Yarra River trail is nearby for morning runs, while the immediate proximity to Hawksburn station and the village ensures effortless city access and a local community atmosphere at your doorstep.

The home’s interior is defined by a commitment to high-end quality, unfolding beneath a seamless interplay of bespoke joinery and rich oak parquetry flooring. At its heart lies an expansive open-plan living and dining domain, warmed by a gas pebble fireplace and anchored by a gourmet stone kitchen appointed with premium Bosch appliances, an integrated Liebherr fridge, and a Vintec wine fridge. Full-height glazing draws abundant natural light throughout the space, extending the living areas to a private landscaped courtyard and a sun-drenched deck, creating a series of serene outdoor retreats perfect for entertaining.

The upper level maintains this cohesive designer aesthetic, where the oak parquetry continues through a luxurious main bedroom featuring a chic ensuite and built-in robes. Accompanied by two further modern bedrooms and a beautifully appointed central bathroom, the accommodation serves as a peaceful retreat from the cosmopolitan surroundings. Comprehensively appointed for modern living with secure keypad entry, CCTV, and ducted heating and cooling, this residence represents an exceptional turnkey opportunity to secure a designer lifestyle in an iconic Melbourne location.

title: 13 Bruce Street, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Sophisticated Family Living in a Premier Pocket
description_plain:
Defined by clean lines, a striking rendered façade, and uncompromising quality, this stylishly appointed family residence offers a lifestyle of effortless luxury. Framed by meticulously manicured, low-maintenance gardens, the home has been designed to capture abundant natural light across both levels, featuring a flowing floorplan that perfectly separates zoned family living.

High ceilings and wide oak floorboards define the generous interior, flowing through the entrance hall to a downstairs guest suite with a walk-in robe, dual-access bathroom, and a large laundry. The upper-level hosts three further bedrooms, including a lavish main suite featuring a walk-in robe and ensuite, a central family bathroom, and a versatile retreat, home office or 5th bedroom.

At the heart of the home, an expansive, full-width family living and dining domain incorporates a sleek kitchen boasting Caesarstone countertops, premium Bosch appliances, and a butler’s pantry. Tall glass sliding doors seamlessly connect this space to a private, hedge-lined deck and garden, creating an idyllic environment for indoor-outdoor entertaining.

Comprehensive appointments include video intercom entry, a security alarm, reverse-cycle air conditioning, ducted vacuum, a rainwater tank, and a remote-controlled double garage with secure internal access and off-street parking.

Situated in a quiet, tree-lined street, moments from the Urban Forest Reserve, East Malvern Station, the new Stonnington Sports Centre and the Malvern Valley Golf Course, this exceptional property offers unparalleled access to Chadstone Shopping Centre and elite schools.
Land size: 333sqm approx.

title: 29 Darling Street, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Bespoke Designer Living in a Private Compound
description_plain:
Breathtaking bespoke living on a grand scale, this impressive five-bedroom, five-bathroom residence stands behind an entirely private and symmetrical solid-brick Victorian façade, revealing a north-facing poolside setting and mature garden surrounds in a prestigious pocket linking Toorak Road’s dining and retail with the Yarra River. Recognised in a leading publication, the interiors reveal unexpected and extraordinary proportions, drawing on European château influences that introduce an exquisite materiality and drama at every turn.
The architectural vision is both romantic and contemporary, highlighting classic structural components, preserving history through painstaking refurbishment, and formulating the perfect mix of modern, custom, and antique elements. Concrete, Venetian plaster, and steel-framed windows and doors sit beneath grand arches, with magnificent ceramic-tiled flooring and gold-toned velvet drapes accentuating the theatre across the formal living and dining domains. An open fireplace enriches the main living room, which flows to the chef’s commercial-grade kitchen and butler’s pantry, defined by rustic red brick, full-height glazing and an inspired verdant backdrop against towering external arches. Northern light fills every space, strengthening the connection to the pool and private garden, where draped leafy tendrils perfectly frame the showstopping alfresco spaces.
Upper-level accommodation comprises four generous en-suite bedrooms, led by a lavish main suite with a sunken Roman bath overlooking the pool below. A second suite provides independence and flexibility, with a sitting room and rear staircase entry, complemented by an independent guest house offering a fifth bedroom, bathroom, and terrace. A gold-wrapped television room or study, a casual sitting area, and an elegant powder room provide further versatility within the main residence, while the basement reveals a wine cellar with SubZero refrigeration, a large laundry and secure parking for four, including a secure garage.
Hydronic panels and slab heating, along with extensive security including an alarm and CCTV, maximise the home’s seclusion and prestige on an elevated site of approximately 649sqm, moments from leading schools, trams, South Yarra Station, The Royal Botanic Gardens and riverside walking trails.

title: 21 Montalto Avenue, Toorak, 3142
heading: Timeless Elegance In Prestigious Montalto Estate
description_plain:
Expressions of Interest close Tue 31 March 3.00pm
Positioned within the exclusive Montalto Estate and moments from Toorak and Hawksburn Villages, this distinguished c1930s residence delivers timeless luxury amid beautifully landscaped gardens and a private pool setting on 939 sqm (approx.).
Beyond its elegant façade, refined interiors of generous scale unfold with formal and informal living zones, open fireplaces and seamless indoor–outdoor entertaining centred around a gourmet Miele-appointed kitchen and sun-filled family domain. Expansive glazing connects living spaces to the serene garden and solar-heated pool, creating an exceptional environment for entertaining and relaxed family living.
Accommodation is superbly appointed with a flexible ground-floor guest suite and an upstairs main retreat with dressing room or study, accompanied by three additional bedrooms and stylish bathrooms.
Walking distance to elite schools, Toorak Station, Orrong Park, trams and village cafés, this refined residence also includes hydronic heating, reverse-cycle air conditioning, alarm security, 2 off street car spaces behind automatic gates and internally accessed double garage.
A rare offering combining heritage grace, privacy and prestige in one of Toorak’s most tightly held addresses.

title: 2 Glenbrook Avenue, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Classic Family Elegance, Parkside Position
description_plain:
Timeless elegance meets solid-brick substance in this beautifully presented and classic family residence, superbly positioned in one of Malvern East’s most picturesque pockets only moments from the entrance to Hedgeley Dene Gardens and a short stroll to Central Park Shopping Village. 
Framed by established, botanic-inspired surrounds, this captivating home opens gracefully through an inviting entrance foyer to a charming light filled living room and separate formal dining room with adjoining northern terrace, modern kitchen, powder room and corner office or fifth bedroom with tranquil views of Glenbrook Avenue, whilst a second level provides four further bedrooms and a stunning Art-Deco inspired family bathroom. A private lower ground level touching the garden offers a unique opportunity to cater for guests, teenage children or multi-generational living with an oversized bedroom, games room, capped open fire-place, cellar and combined laundry and second bathroom. Double garage, heating and cooling. Land size: 750sqm approx.

title: 7 Joy Street, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Sleek Design Meets Vibrant Village Lifestyle
description_plain:
Behind a sleek rendered façade lies a sanctuary of breathtaking architectural vision. Completely reimagined to exacting standards, this exquisite split-level residence offers a sophisticated, gallery-like aesthetic defined by dramatic high ceilings, strategic floor-to-ceiling windows, and elegant curved walls that flood the home with natural light.

Designed for seamless indoor-outdoor living, the home revolves around a sun-drenched, north-facing courtyard oasis, perfect for tranquil relaxation or grand entertaining. The ground level features a striking slate-tiled entrance, a generous bedroom, powder room, and a magnificent open-plan living and dining area. The gourmet kitchen is a masterpiece of design, showcasing sleek stone countertops and premium Bosch and Smeg appliances, with a second courtyard and laundry completing this level.

Upstairs, a substantial north-facing retreat offers stunning elevated views across South Yarra to the CBD skyline. Accommodation comprises two additional bedrooms, including the main suite with a walk-in robe, built-in robes, and a luxurious ensuite. A family bathroom, second powder room, and a versatile home office opening to a private balcony complete the upper level.
Enhanced by premium appointments throughout, featuring a remote double garage with internal access, Jetmaster fireplace, reverse cycle heating and cooling, double-glazed windows, this is the epitome of low-maintenance luxury.

Situated in a vibrant lifestyle pocket, this home offers unparalleled convenience just moments from the refined boutiques, cafes, and restaurants of Hawksburn Village, Chapel Street, and Toorak Road. With easy CBD access, nearby tram and train options, and close proximity to parklands and elite schools, this is an outstanding opportunity for low-maintenance luxury in an exclusive locale.

title: The Residence, 8 Devorgilla Avenue, Toorak, 3142
heading: An Idyllic Toorak Retreat with Classic English Influence
description_plain:
Positioned within one of Toorak’s most prestigious tree-lined avenues, The Residence, 8 Devorgilla Av, Toorak presents a refined Georgian-inspired home, recently refreshed to deliver immediate comfort and understated sophistication in a quiet, highly convenient setting.

Privately set behind a high-walled garden courtyard, the home opens to light-filled interiors of generous proportion. A wide entrance foyer with powder room introduces elegant living and dining domains, complemented by a separate sitting room, creating flexibility for both formal entertaining and relaxed day-to-day living. The kitchen is well-appointed and functional, seamlessly connected to the home’s living spaces.

Recent enhancements elevate the home throughout, including fresh paint, new carpets, updated plantation shutters, stylish new light fittings, and a renewed laundry and powder room, ensuring a turnkey opportunity with nothing further required.

Outdoors, two beautifully private garden courtyards offer peaceful retreats for entertaining or quiet enjoyment, with the added comfort of a retractable awning providing shade and versatility year-round.

Upstairs accommodation is well-considered, with a spacious main bedroom featuring extensive robes and ensuite, accompanied by two additional bedrooms - each offering flexibility for family living or a dedicated study - served by a bright central bathroom.

Additional features include ducted heating, reverse cycle air conditioning, security doors, and the rare advantage of off-street parking for two, including a lock-up garage.

Enjoying a quiet yet connected position, the home is within easy walking distance of Malvern Road trams, Toorak railway station, local cafés and boutiques, while moments from Toorak Village, Toorak Park and some of Melbourne’s leading schools - delivering an exceptional lifestyle in a blue-chip locale.

title: 48 Banole Avenue, Prahran, 3181
heading: A Complete Charmer in a Coveted Pocket
description_plain:
Providing the rich character of the era, contemporary style and an inner urban location to aspire to, this classic solid brick Edwardian residence’s well refurbished dimensions deliver instant lifestyle appeal advantaged by coveted off street parking.
The quiet tree-lined one-way street ensures tranquillity and privacy yet only moments away there are a host of cafes, restaurants and boutiques as well as transport options and leafy Victoria and Lumley Gardens. A traditional L-shaped hallway featuring high ceilings and timber floors introduces three double bedrooms, one with a fireplace, and a stylish bathroom. The generous living and dining room with a fireplace is adjacent to a sleek modern stone kitchen. The private landscaped west-facing garden oasis is an idyllic spot for relaxed outdoor dining and entertaining while also offering space for secure off-street parking easily accessed from Packington Street.
Immaculately presented to immediately enjoy, whilst still offering great scope to further update STCA, it includes ducted heating, RC/air-conditioning and a Euro-laundry. Land Size: 200sqm approx.

title: 39a James Street, Windsor, 3181
heading: Windsor House by Cera Stribley – 2 Stunning Homes
description_plain:
A creative re-interpretation of a warehouse conversion, this sensational 4 ½ year old residence designed by renowned architects Cera Stribley succeeds in presenting a sophisticated inner urban showcase of impressive proportions, inspired designer flair and bespoke finishes. Also included is a 2-bedroom garden residence with frontage on Primrose Street with its own title and provides exceptional flexibility for multi-generational living or passive income. 
A dramatic street profile of recycled bricks and charred timber makes way for the interior dimensions where soft, neutral tones, soaring 3m ceilings and doors and the strategic placement of windows creates an expansive, light-filled and entirely private sanctuary of calm. Beneath European oak ceilings, micro-cement floors flow through the expansive open plan living and dining room with feature timber joinery, a gas log fire, built in bench seating and a balcony. The unified palate is maintained through the sublime gourmet kitchen where porcelain benches, Bosch appliances, integrated fridge/freezer and a butler’s pantry ensure culinary efficiency. The beautiful main bedroom with built in custom robes, a sleek designer ensuite and balcony with outdoor shower is matched by two additional double bedrooms with built in robes and desks, a balcony and stylish bathroom. Crowning this stunning residence is a 100sqm approx. rooftop terrace offering wonderful views over Prahan to the city. With a fabulous BBQ pavilion with fridge and built in dining, it’s an unbeatable venue for outdoor dining, cinema nights and entertaining. 
Curated with the same level of finish and designer style, the adjacent garden residence comprises a living/dining space with gourmet kitchen, two bedrooms, two bathrooms, a powder-room and laundry.
With all the restaurants, cafes and bars that make Chapel Street such a drawcard only metres away as well as Windsor station, trams, schools and Chris Gahan Reserve, this remarkable domain is an unmatched lifestyle haven. Securely appointed with CCTV, video intercom and gated entrance, it also includes underfloor hydronic heating, zoned RC/air-conditioning, remote blinds, powder-room, laundry with drying cupboard and courtyard, double glazing, dimmable lights, irrigation, ample storage and internally accessed oversized double garage.

title: 6/125 High Street, Prahran, 3181
heading: An Icon of Warehouse Living
description_plain:
One of only six and steps from Greville Street and Prahran Market and the areas dynamic cafes, restaurants, and fitness studios, this much sort after designer apartment within an iconic warehouse conversion establishes a compelling foothold in one of Melbourne’s most exciting lifestyle locations. Spanning two sunlit north-facing levels with generous proportions inside and out, secure lift access and a private double remote garage, it affords genuine privacy and exclusivity while keeping every lifestyle advantage close.

Past the central gardens, the luxurious interior immediately captivates, with herringbone oak floors, original steel-framed windows, soaring ceilings, and full-height stacked sliders that extend the main living and dining domain to the central terrace. A northern aspect draws natural light across both levels through a dramatic open-air void, linking the alfresco areas with the high-end kitchen, finished with marble and stone surfaces, a butler’s pantry, and a complete suite of Bosch appliances, including twin ovens.

A fitted study on the upper landing makes excellent use of the space, preceding three robed bedrooms. The main suite overlooks the courtyard from its private balcony, creating an ambient setting with a gas fire, walk-in fitted robe, and a marble-detailed ensuite with underfloor heating, and urban lights visible from the twin shower. Two additional bedrooms enjoy all-day sun, motorised blinds, and built-in robes, and are served by a central bathroom with a freestanding bath.

With Prahran Station, Chapel Street’s mix of cafés, wine bars, restaurants, trams and fashion boutiques just steps away, and the Royal Botanic Gardens, Fawkner Park and Albert Park Lake all within easy reach, it delivers a first-class lifestyle, supported by ducted heating and cooling, keyless entry, a laundry with powder room and extensive storage.

title: 302/2 Cromwell Road, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Sophisticated Boutique Living
description_plain:
One of only two on the third level, this sensational sun-drenched boutique residence’s bespoke designer style and seamless indoor-outdoor flow delivers enviable lock up and leave living in an acclaimed lifestyle hub.
Oak floors flow through the entrance hall to the expansive open plan living and dining room awash with northern and eastern light and warmed by a gas log fire. Two sets of stacking glass sliders open the living spaces to a wraparound terrace and entertaining deck, providing idyllic spaces for al fresco dining and relaxing. The stunning gourmet kitchen caters to every requirement with its Carrara marble benches, Miele appliances and integrated fridge/freezer. Also opening to the balcony, the main bedroom with built in robe and designer ensuite is accompanied by a second robed bedroom, a home office or third bedroom with desk and robes and a stylish bathroom. 
Walking distance to Hawksburn Village, Chapel St and Commercial Rd’s restaurants and shops, Prahran market, trams, schools, Hawksburn station and Victoria Gardens, it includes lift access, video intercom, RC/air-conditioning, remote blinds, Euro-laundry, two storage cages and two basement car spaces.

title: 129 Bendigo Street, Prahran, 3181
heading: Style, Space and Sophistication
description_plain:
The inspired result of an award-winning design by Sync Architects, this stunning inner urban luxury domain brilliantly adapts to all the requirements of modern family living whether entertaining, working from home or simply relaxing.
Against a lush backdrop of greenery, the expansive open plan living and dining room features wide oak floors, high ceilings, a gas log fire, remote blinds and a sleek gourmet kitchen appointed with stone benches and Miele appliances. Full width sliding glass opens the living space to the tranquil west facing landscaped courtyard, a lush oasis for outdoor living. Generous accommodation on the second level comprises the beautiful main bedroom with a designer en suite and walk in robe, two additional robed bedrooms, a home office and stylish bathroom. A sensational retreat on the top level opens fully to a large al fresco terrace surrounded by planter boxes and offering wonderful views over the rooftops of Prahran. Complete with an outdoor shower, wet bar and powder-room, it is an idyllic setting for year-round indoor-outdoor entertaining.
Enviably situated just a short walk to Chapel Street, Prahran Market, Prahran Pool, Hawksburn Village, Prahran and Hawksburn stations, trams and a range of schools, it includes solar power, an alarm, video intercom, CCTV, ducted and underfloor heating, RC/air-conditioning, second powder-room, laundry, ducted vacuuming, irrigation, ample storage and an internally accessed garage with bike racks and storage.

title: 1/3 Douglas Street, Toorak, 3142
heading: "Caserta" - Bespoke Design and Boutique Luxury
description_plain:
Inspect by Private Appointment 

Exclusively positioned in a tightly held boutique enclave of five luxury residences and located on the edge of Toorak Village, this spectacular ground level garden residence reflects the signature designer flair, quality and attention to detail of architect Christopher Doyle.
Sophisticated finishes including oak floors, an abundance of marble and 3m ceilings distinguish the impressively proportioned interior dimensions. A marble foyer introduces the sumptuous main bedroom with marble en suite, bath, walk in/built in robes and private terrace, two additional king bedrooms each with marble en suite and robes and a refined home office. The gracious sitting room with a gas fireplace flows through to the  formal dining room and generous informal living area. The interconnecting spaces, conducive to both relaxed living or indoor-outdoor entertaining, all open to an expansive paved courtyard with garden beds and lush green surrounds. The state-of-the-art kitchen is impeccably appointed with marble benches, a suite of Miele appliances including steam oven, coffee maker and integrated fridge/freezer and a butler’s pantry with wine fridge. 
Just a short stroll to Como Park, Royal South Yarra Tennis Club, the Yarra River and excellent schools, it is finished to an elite level with alarm, video intercom, remote blinds, zoned RC/air-conditioning, heated bathroom floors, Sonos, TVs, powder-room, laundry, ample storage, basement storeroom and three car-spaces.

title: 2B Sorrett Avenue, Malvern, 3144
heading: Elegant family home or downsizer retreat in a prized Malvern pocket
description_plain:
This stunning solid brick 3-bedroom/3-bathroom contemporary residence is brilliantly located in magnificent Sorrett Avenue, offering superbly proportioned dimensions and an idyllic lifestyle oasis. Nestled within lush, peaceful garden surrounds, the home is only metres from the coveted retail and restaurant precincts of High Street and Glenferrie Road. Multiple train and tram lines ensure public transport access in all directions, including to the many prestigious schools in the area.

Behind a tranquil walled forecourt, the wide entrance hall flows through to a gracious sitting and dining room with fireplace. The superbly appointed gourmet stone kitchen leads to an oversized laundry with abundant storage space. Step outside to the wonderfully private northeast facing garden sanctuary with a large paved entertaining terrace bathed in sunshine.

Cleverly designed accommodation adapts effortlessly to any number of family requirements. A light-filled double bedroom with walk in robe and stylish ensuite is on the ground level, as is a second double bedroom with robes and adjacent bathroom. Upstairs is a lavishly proportioned main bedroom with walk in robe and designer ensuite complete with double basins, showers and underfloor heating. An expansive adult retreat with double glass doors opens to a second sun-soaked relaxation space, completing the picture.

The location speaks for itself: exceptional public transport, prestigious schools, and beautiful Malvern Gardens within easy reach. The home is finished with ducted heating and air-conditioning for year-round comfort, automatic irrigation to keep the gardens looking their best, and a double garage. Inspect and you’ll understand immediately why homes on Sorrett Avenue are so rarely offered.

title: 1 & 2/526 Punt Road, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Melbourne’s Most Iconic Corner
description_plain:
This blue-chip pair of period style apartments extends a plethora of opportunities. Invest, refurbish or strategically hold one of Melbourne’s most iconic corner positions. Ideally located with The Botanical Gardens, MCG, AAMI Park and Yarra River Trails just minutes away and close to South Yarra & Richmond Station, Sports Precinct, CBD, an array of elite schools and the best of both South Yarra, Richmond and Inner Melbourne at your fingertips.
Both two-bedroom apartments feature house size proportions, well sized kitchens, central bathrooms, separate dining and glorious living rooms, featuring superb rounded bay windows and vistas toward the Yarra River
Enjoying great retained period character throughout, the block provides exciting scope to improve and renovate further (STCA) and is enhanced by 3 garages and generous garden surrounds.

title: 1/6 Cawkwell Street, Malvern, 3144
heading: Elegance and Convenience
description_plain:
Impeccably presented and privately positioned, this freestanding dual-level residence delivers refined, low-maintenance living at the front of a boutique development, just moments from vibrant village amenities.

A secure gate opens to a European-inspired paved courtyard, perfectly oriented for alfresco dining and relaxation. Inside, high ceilings and quality finishes create an immediate sense of sophistication. A formal lounge with a Heat & Glo fireplace is complemented by a separate sitting area with a flexible work-from-home space, while a light-filled family living and dining zone forms the heart of the home. The well-appointed kitchen features a waterfall island breakfast bar, generous storage and premium Miele appliances. A large laundry, guest powder room and internal garage access complete the ground level.

Upstairs, the spacious main suite includes plantation shutters, walk-in robes and a stylish ensuite with dual vanities. Two additional robed bedrooms are served by a family bathroom and separate WC, while ducted heating and cooling ensures year-round comfort.

Enjoy outstanding lifestyle convenience with Malvern Hill Village, Glenferrie Road shops and cafés nearby, along with Malvern and Tooronga Stations, trams, Malvern 

Public Gardens and the Harold Holt Swim Centre. Zoned for sought-after schools including St Joseph’s Primary, De La Salle College and Caulfield Grammar’s Malvern Campus.

Elegant, secure and superbly located, this home offers effortless living in a highly desirable Malvern setting.

title: 2/9 Wilmot Street, Malvern East, 3145
other_features: Air Conditioning,Alarm System,Water tank,Built In Robes
heading: Boutique Family Villa, Ardrie Park Estate Address
description_plain:
Architecturally designed and located within the prized Ardrie Park Estate, this new three-bedroom residence delivers exceptional contemporary family living in a quiet, low-traffic pocket, one of only three villas in an exclusive boutique setting. A refined façade and crisp landscaping introduce oak floors and soaring ceilings, creating an immediate sense of space, calm and impeccable quality.

The home opens to a beautifully scaled open-plan living and dining domain, where full-height glazing enhances the airy proportions. A sleek kitchen features Tundra Marble benchtops, warm timber accents, a generous island breakfast bar, and premium Smeg appliances including a 90cm oven, induction cooktop and dishwasher, all supported by impressive storage for effortless day-to-day living. Sliding glass doors extend the main zone to a private north-facing bluestone terrace and garden space, ideal for children, pets and easy entertaining. A pristine laundry with excellent storage and an adjacent powder room add daily practicality.

Upstairs, a central landing with study nook leads to a generous main suite with expansive built-in robes and a luxe ensuite featuring dual vanities and an oversized rain shower. Two additional robed bedrooms are served by a stylish family bathroom. Refrigerated heating and cooling, an alarm system, irrigation, a 2,500L water tank, abundant storage, and a double garage complete a home where every inclusion feels considered and purposeful.

Enjoy the lifestyle advantage of Ardrie Park at the end of the street for playground time, tennis, weekend football and dog walks, with Waverley Road trams, Carnegie Station, Chadstone Shopping Centre and Koornang Road’s cafés and dining all close by. With well-regarded local schools nearby, this is a rare opportunity to secure brand new architectural living that feels peacefully tucked away yet seamlessly connected for modern family life.

title: G03/1220 Malvern Road, Malvern, 3144
heading: Boutique Living with Private Garden Entry
description_plain:
Positioned within a peaceful, leafy garden setting in a vibrant lifestyle pocket, this exceptional ground-floor residence in the boutique Spring development presents an ideal lock-and-leave opportunity. Defined by its generous contemporary proportions and tightly held position within the building, it also enjoys the rare benefit of direct pedestrian access via Spring Road.
 
Designed by C. Kairouz Architects with interiors by CoLAB Design Studio, the home has been thoughtfully crafted for effortless, low-maintenance living. Expansive living and dining areas extend seamlessly to private landscaped gardens, while engineered oak flooring and oversized windows invite natural light and greenery into the space. The kitchen is tailored for entertaining, featuring stone benchtops, a secondary island, premium Miele appliances including a gas cooktop, a wine fridge, extensive storage, and bespoke oak-veneer cabinetry with an integrated bar.
 
A gas fireplace adds warmth to the living area, with sliding doors opening to a sunken terrace surrounded by established plantings, a Japanese maple, secure gated street access and a concealed putting green, creating a distinctive outdoor retreat. Accommodation is thoughtfully zoned, with two rear bedrooms with built-in robes, one opening directly to the garden—serviced by a spacious bathroom finished with mosaic tiling and an inset bath. The main suite is privately positioned and features mirrored robes, a walk-in robe and a dual-vanity ensuite with twin showers.
 
Additional highlights include reverse-cycle ducted heating and cooling, a European laundry, alarm system, security cameras, video intercom and secure fob entry. Secure basement parking provides two car spaces with above-bonnet storage, plus a separate storage cage. The location offers exceptional lifestyle convenience, moments from the Route 72 tram, Tooronga railway station, Malvern Gardens, Malvern Central School and the vibrant shopping and dining along Glenferrie Road.

title: 39-41 Hopetoun Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Rostrevor - A Landmark Estate Poised for the Future
description_plain:
A landmark along one of Toorak’s most prestigious boulevards, “Rostrevor”, an Old English-style residence designed by Cedric Ballantyne in 1934, offers immediate family living alongside plans for an extraordinary, yet sympathetic architectural revival. Complete with approved plans and permits by the acclaimed Christopher Doyle, and landscape design by Paul Bangay, the opportunity is defined by its significant 1,665sqm approx. landholding and wide frontage, with every stage prepared for transformation.

Period craftsmanship and magnificent detail remain intact, with terracotta shingle roofing, decorative ceilings and generous proportions reinforcing the home’s original integrity. Multiple formal and informal living areas are oriented to the north-west, drawing outlooks across the pool and established gardens, whilst a recently renovated porcelain kitchen with European appliances offers instant appeal. Five bedrooms and three and a half bathrooms across two levels, including a separate guest cottage and upper-level main suite with a balcony and sitting room, provide flexibility for evolving family needs, whilst features include lower-level garaging, heating/cooling, and a cellar.

The proposed new design creates a residence of incomparable presence, incorporating the Tudor beauty of the original homestead while transforming it with seven en-suite bedrooms, expansive entertaining areas, a north-south tennis court, and a resort-style pool, supported by underground parking for seven vehicles. Additional inclusions, such as a lift, golf simulator, wine tasting room, cellar, and gym, enhance the vision, affording a peerless opportunity in an irreplaceable setting, close to leading private schools, Toorak and Hawksburn Villages, multiple tram routes, the Monash and Yarra River trails. Land: 1,665sqm (approx.)

title: 61 Finch Street, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Federation Elegance, Family Excellence
description_plain:
Magnificently capturing the grandeur of the era, this distinguished c1900 solid brick Federation residence delivers exceptional family appeal, breathtaking landscaped gardens, pool surrounds and its prized Gascoigne Estate position.

A wide reception hall with Baltic pine floors, soaring ceilings and exquisite stained glass creates an unforgettable introduction, flowing through to a refined sitting room adorned with a feature fireplace and elegant bay window. The luxurious main bedroom is a private retreat, complete with fireplace, walk-in robe and designer ensuite, accompanied by a stunning second bedroom with fireplace and bay window, two further generous bedrooms with built-in robes, a fully fitted study and a beautifully appointed family bathroom.

Rich Spotted Gum floors extend through the expansive open plan living and dining domain, where a sublime European kitchen showcases stone benchtops, soft-close cabinetry and premium Gaggenau appliances. Bi-fold doors seamlessly connect indoors to a spectacular hedged north-west facing garden, featuring a solar-heated pool and inviting alfresco terrace, perfect for relaxed family living and memorable entertaining. A versatile double garage further enhances the flexibility of the outdoor domain, with five-car off-road additional parking.

Impressively set on one level, additional features include alarm and automatic/remote security gates, powder room, laundry, central heating and cooling throughout, solar panels and inverters, plus plenty of additional storage space. Ideally located within a short distance of Melbourne’s leading schools including Caulfield Grammar, Sacré Cœur, Korowa and Malvern Primary, this is a rare opportunity to secure a landmark Federation home of enduring elegance and contemporary comfort.

title: 34 Motherwell Street, South Yarra, 3141
heading: A New benchmark for Luxury in South Yarra
description_plain:
Expressions of Interest Close 2nd April at 3pm 

An architectural triumph of contemporary design, this newly completed North-facing freestanding Victorian has been transformed into a state-of-the-art entertainer, defined by exceptional quality, lift-connected interiors and a private north-south poolside setting on the cusp of Hawksburn Village. It delivers secure parking for up to five vehicles, with ROW access and an uncompromising approach to five-star living in one of South Yarra’s most desirable streets.

A striking façade introduces breathtaking interiors arranged across two generous levels, illuminated by skylights that draw natural light through every zone. A dramatic sculptural staircase, crowned by a circular skylight, becomes a defining centrepiece, sitting beautifully within the open living and social domains. Expansive family and dining areas are enhanced by an electric fireplace set within Venetian plaster, while full-height stacked sliding glass doors open to a private outdoor sanctuary with a north-facing pool and a plumbed BBQ kitchen.

Taj Mahal quartzite surfaces distinguish the main kitchen, appointed with premium Miele appliances and integrated refrigeration, supported by a full butler’s kitchen with induction cooking, integrated microwave, warming drawer and a second dishwasher with provision for additional refrigeration.

Impeccable zoning ensures long-term flexibility, with four robed bedrooms including a choice of main suites on each level, five bathrooms in total, and potential for a fifth bedroom, third living area or home office. Parisi tapware, marble lighting and underfloor heating define each bathroom, including the opulent upper main suite, which embodies a lavish haven with a designer dressing room and twin ensuite with a freestanding bath.

A large laundry and mudroom, 400+ bottle wine cellar with exquisite marble detail, extensive security system with CCTV, secure intercom and alarm nod to the uncompromising quality of this home. Featuring Italian kiln-fired bricks, Italian-imported lift, reverse-cycle multi-zone heating/cooling, a substantial 6m x 10.7m four-car garage via ROW with additional secure parking to the front, all highlight a peerless address, positioned within moments of Hawksburn Station, trams, leading schools, Victoria Gardens and Como Park. Back by a builder’s warranty from a premier Master Builder, this modern transformation spared no expense nor detail and offers a truly unique product to the luxury market.

title: 5/5 Woorigoleen Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Nicholas Day Classic in Sought After Toorak Locale
description_plain:
Designed with enduring classical refinement by architect Nicholas Day, this light-filled ground floor residence showcases soaring ceilings and a seamless connection to its outdoor surrounds, creating an effortless setting for alfresco living.

Peacefully positioned towards the rear of a boutique building in one of Toorak’s most picturesque tree-lined streets — just moments from the prestigious homes of Clendon Road — this single level three bedroom, two bathroom residence offers secure, low maintenance living in the heart of Melbourne’s most exclusive suburb.

Thoughtfully designed with excellent separation between the living domains and bedroom accommodation, the home will appeal to downsizers, families, or those who value timeless architecture enhanced by quality appointments and generous proportions. Wide oak floorboards, full height doors and impeccable finishes form a sophisticated foundation, while picture rails provide the perfect backdrop for curated artwork.

The elegant living and dining room extends through glass doors to a captivating north facing courtyard garden, inviting year round indoor-outdoor entertaining. A beautifully appointed kitchen featuring Caesarstone benchtops and Miele appliances flows effortlessly into a versatile adjoining space suited to both formal gatherings and relaxed occasions.

Privately zoned, the main bedroom enjoys garden access, a walk-in robe and a stylish ensuite. Two additional robed bedrooms are positioned along a separate hallway and share a central bathroom.

Additional features include secure entry, integrated heating and cooling, a European laundry, blockout blinds, downlights, lift access to the basement car park and a private double garage. Superbly located, the home is just minutes from Toorak Road trams, close to the Village’s boutiques and cafes, and offers easy access to many of Melbourne’s leading schools.

title: 22 Seymour Avenue, Armadale, 3143
heading: Edwardian Elegance in an Elite Address
description_plain:
Serenely nestled behind a high hedged garden, this stunning solid brick Edwardian residence’s impressively renovated dimensions provide the perfect blend of original elegance, contemporary style and spaces for effortless indoor-outdoor living and entertaining.
Timber floors and ornate ceilings are displayed through the inviting home office and the adjacent sitting room, both with open fires. The expansive open plan living/dining room and gourmet kitchen appointed with stone benches, Smeg/Bosch appliances and a butler’s pantry/laundry open through double doors to a private bluestone paved northeast facing courtyard with leafy surrounds and a double garage. Two double bedrooms with robes and a stylish bathroom are on the ground level while a lavish main bedroom suite with robes and a designer en suite enjoys upstairs tranquillity.
In a highly sought after location only metres to High St and Glenferrie Rd’s renowned retail and restaurant precinct, elite schools, Armadale station, trams and Union St Park, it includes an alarm video intercom, plantation shutters, ducted heating/cooling, powder-room, solar panels and storeroom. Land size 354sqm approx.
