title: 27 Jackson Street, Toorak, 3142
heading: Concrete Curtain
description_plain:
A private sanctuary of sculptural calm, Concrete Curtain is a striking architectural residence designed by acclaimed FGRArchitects and a finalist in the World Architectural Festival. Defined by rhythmic pleated concrete, expansive glazing and refined natural materials, the home balances fortress-Like privacy with light-filled openness and seamless indoor-outdoor connection.
Thoughtfully arranged across split levels, the layout delivers multiple car accommodation, a dedicated office, flowing family living and dining zones, and a showpiece kitchen finished in rare Seafoam Ocean-Waves quartzite. Upstairs, 4 beautifully proportioned bedrooms with ensuites, highlighted by a serene main bedroom suite with dressing room crowned by an occulus skylight, stunning ensuite and library culminate in a serene rear master suite capturing leafy borrowed views to the north.
Travertine flooring, curated lighting and a central light-well courtyard enhance the home's sense of volume, tranquillity and tactile luxury.
Engaging the streetscape with quiet confidence, this is a rare opportunity to secure a design-led residence of architectural significance and enduring sophistication.

title: 1/14 Lascelles Avenue, Toorak, 3142
heading: Luxury with Large Private Garden Terrace
description_plain: Visionary design by renowned Melbourne architect Christopher Doyle defines the rare luxury of single level, ground floor living positioned in prestigious Lascelles Manor. Bathed in natural light, this refined residence offers an exceptional sense of privacy, tranquillity and lifestyle ease in a coveted Toorak Village setting. Beautifully proportioned open plan living and dining spaces are underscored by elegant herringbone timber floors and French doors that extend to a north and west facing terrace and beautifully landscaped garden, where serenely private outdoor connection awaits, ideal for everyday enjoyment as well as entertaining. The kitchen is perfectly appointed with a suite of premium Miele appliances including two ovens, integrated fridge and freezer, wine fridge and dishwasher. Deep marble benchtops, outstanding storage, a central island and a walk in butler’s pantry complete the practicalities. Accommodation is equally impressive with both spacious bedrooms enjoying garden access via sliding doors. The primary bedroom comes with a generous walk through robe and a luxurious ensuite with marble finishes, twin vanity, separate bath and shower. A second bathroom extends the flexibility. Additional highlights include wool carpet, double glazing, heating and cooling, dedicated laundry, excellent internal storage, external blinds, secure video intercom entry, two secure car parks, two storage cages and resident gym facilities. Set within walking distance of Toorak Village, this is the epitome of tranquil convenience with timeless appeal.

title: 5/66 Williams Road, Prahran, 3181
heading: Brand-New Architect-Designed Townhome in a Private Prahran Setting.
description_plain:
Tucked quietly toward the rear of the boutique Prime Prahran development, this architect-designed townhome delivers a rare balance of privacy, contemporary design and unbeatable convenience.

Designed by award-winning Ascui & Co, the residence forms part of an exclusive collection of just six sophisticated townhomes, created for modern inner-city living. Positioned away from the street, the home enjoys a peaceful setting while remaining just moments from the energy and lifestyle of Prahran.

Inside, northern light-filled interiors unfold across multiple levels, with open-plan living and dining connecting seamlessly to a private terrace — creating the perfect setting for both entertaining and everyday living. Natural timber flooring, stone finishes and clean architectural lines create a warm yet refined aesthetic.

Brand-new and never before occupied, the home offers the advantages of contemporary design and low-maintenance living in one of Melbourne’s most established and desirable neighbourhoods.

Home Features

• Architecturally designed townhome by Ascui & Co
• Boutique development of just six residences
• Quiet rear position within the development
• Northern light-filled open-plan living and dining
• Seamless connection to a private north-facing terrace
• Two Generous ensuite bedrooms
• Secure lock-up garage with internal access
• Brand-new residence, never before occupied
• Energy-efficient design and contemporary finishes throughout
• 10 year Structural Guarantee
• 52 week Defect Period 
 

Lifestyle & Location

• Walk to High Street cafés, restaurants and boutiques
• 95 Walkscore
• Easy access to Prahran, Windsor and Armadale train stations
• Multiple tram routes nearby for effortless CBD access
• Surrounded by parks, fitness studios and everyday amenities

A rare opportunity to secure a brand-new architect-designed townhome in one of Prahran’s most connected locations, offering the perfect combination of lifestyle, privacy and long-term value. Flexible settlement terms available.

title: 36/380 Toorak Road, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Elegant South Yarra Garden Living
description_plain:
Set within the tightly held Kingsfold complex, this beautifully appointed three-bedroom, two-bathroom residence delivers spacious proportions, leafy outlooks and an expansive terrace retreat in one of Melbourne’s most desirable lifestyle precincts.
Enjoying a peaceful position overlooking the building’s manicured communal gardens and tranquil water feature, the apartment offers a wonderful sense of privacy and connection to nature. A generous living and dining zone is filled with natural light and flows seamlessly to the well-equipped kitchen, featuring quality appliances, ample cabinetry and excellent preparation space, perfect for entertaining or relaxed everyday living.
A versatile third bedroom or study with built-in storage provides flexibility for working from home or accommodating guests. The serene main bedroom includes a walk-in robe and private ensuite, opening directly to the oversized terrace to create a calming indoor–outdoor sanctuary. A second bedroom with built-in robe is serviced by a central bathroom, while a separate laundry enhances everyday convenience.
Additional highlights include secure intercom entry, climate control, plentiful storage, three car spaces and access to the building’s gym and beautifully maintained gardens.
Perfectly positioned on Toorak Road in vibrant South Yarra, this quiet yet connected address is moments from boutique shopping and cafés at Toorak Village and Chapel Street, with parklands at Como Park and walking trails along the Yarra River nearby. Excellent public transport options ensure effortless access to the Melbourne CBD, elite schools and the very best of Melbourne living.
A superb combination of space, serenity and blue-chip convenience, this exceptional residence presents an outstanding opportunity to secure a refined home in the heart of South Yarra.

title: 1 Manning Road, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Exceptional Opportunity on the Estate
description_plain:
Step inside this enchanting c1914 Edwardian home, where timeless period charm beautifully blends with contemporary sophistication. A wide, welcoming entrance hall sets the tone, leading to formal sitting and dining rooms, each featuring original fireplaces that add character and warmth. The main bedroom is a luxurious retreat with a private ensuite, complemented by two additional bedrooms and a versatile study or rumpus room.

At the heart of the home, a thoughtfully designed kitchen showcases quality finishes and flows seamlessly into the open-plan living and dining area, which spills effortlessly onto a private north-facing alfresco, ideal for entertaining or enjoying peaceful family moments. Sunlight fills every corner, enhancing the sense of space and highlighting the home's elegant period details alongside modern conveniences.

Additional features include ducted heating, air-conditioning, generous storage, an irrigation system, a double garage, and a mature, landscaped garden that offers both beauty and privacy. With its perfect balance of charm, functionality, and luxury, this home provides an exceptional lifestyle in a timeless setting.

title: 14 Sycamore Street, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Exceptional Family Appeal
description_plain:
Reflecting the period streetscape, this captivating four-bedroom/three-bathroom contemporary residence makes an instant and lasting impact with its generosity of space, multiple living zones, contemporary elegance and low-maintenance ease.
Rich timber floors add wonderful character while 3.1m ceilings amplify the sense of space in the broad central hallway, generous sitting and formal dining room and a separate living room/media room with gas log fire. The expansive dining area with a gourmet kitchen appointed with stone benches and a 90cm Smeg oven opens through bi-fold doors to a large, decked north-east-facing courtyard with built in seating. A double bedroom with robes, a bay window overlooking the picturesque front garden and stylish bathroom are on the ground level. Upstairs, the main bedroom with lavish ensuite and spacious walk-in robes is accompanied by two additional robed bedrooms, a modern bathroom with bath and an inviting rumpus with balcony enjoying leafy views.
Idyllically situated close to East Malvern station, several schools, Waverley Road shops and cafes, Chadstone Shopping Centre, Waverley Oval and the Urban Forest, it includes an alarm, CCTV, ducted heating/cooling, RC/air-conditioners, ceiling fans, laundry, water tank, storage and double garage.

title: 3 Walnut Street, Malvern, 3144
heading: Peerless Executive Luxury
description_plain:
A showcase of contemporary refinement and luxury designed by acclaimed architect Nicholas Day, this stunning four-five-bedroom/three-bathroom residence’s generously proportioned and versatile dimensions deliver an incomparable executive lifestyle in a highly sought after locale.
Framed by a lush, landscaped garden, the travertine entrance foyer introduces an expansive sitting and formal dining room appointed with plantation shutters, chevron timber floors and a gas log fire. Bi-fold doors seamlessly connect the room to a beautiful north-facing landscaped courtyard with privacy ensured by surrounding Manchurian Pears. Providing an idyllic indoor-outdoor flow for entertaining, the light-filled family room and sleek gourmet kitchen with stone benches and Miele appliances also access the courtyard through an additional set of bi-fold doors. Intelligently designed accommodation offers the flexibility of a home office or guest bedroom on the ground level accompanied by a stylish bathroom while skylit stairs lead up to the lavish main bedroom with walk through robes and a marble designer en suite, three additional robed bedrooms and a stylish marble bathroom with bath and separate powder-room. Featuring an epoxy resin floor, the large basement double garage with wine cellar and storage also allows space for a gym. 
Exclusively situated in a prized and tranquil pocket yet only moments to Tooronga station, Malvern Village’s shops and restaurants, trams, elite schools such as Scotch College, St Kevins College, Lauriston Girls’ School, Loreto Mandeville Hall, and Bialik College, Menzies Reserve, Gardiner’s Creek bike path and the Monash freeway, it includes RC/air-conditioning throughout, video intercom and laundry.

title: 53 Paxton Street, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Prime Development Opportunity in Malvern East
description_plain:
Positioned in one of Malvern East’s most prestigious pockets, this exceptional north-facing 780sqm allotment (18.3m frontage) presents a rare opportunity to build your dream home or explore multi-development potential. Perfectly located on the doorstep of Central Park and Village, and just moments from the tranquil Hedgeley Dene Gardens, this site offers an enviable lifestyle with cafés, shops, and transport all within easy reach.

Currently occupied by a charming four-bedroom period weatherboard home in original condition, the property offers immediate renovation potential or a blank canvas for redevelopment (STCA). With such generous frontage and a coveted north orientation, this is a rare chance to secure a prime piece of Malvern East real estate with endless possibilities. No heritage overlay with existing plans drawn up for a single dwelling residence.

title: 15 Illowa Street, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Architectural Excellence with Rooftop Sanctuary & Private Lift
description_plain:
Inspect by Private Appointment – Please contact Jack Nicol directly

Amid a coveted tree-lined setting on the cusp of vibrant lifestyle precincts, this brand-new multi-level residence is the result of an elite collaboration between Developers c/o Locale, Builders M+A Group, Architects DKO, and landscape Florian Wild, united by a vision to deliver luxury that honours environmental responsibility alongside uncompromising quality.

The exterior speaks to this philosophy through Eco Timber's Shou Sugi Ban cladding, a traditional Japanese charring technique that yields blackened timber façades of remarkable durability, complemented by deep window shrouds and Colorbond black detailing that embed the architecture within its leafy surrounds. A private lift connects three levels, beginning at the secure garage and rising through thoughtfully conceived spaces. The ground floor hosts a guest bedroom with built-in robes and a full bathroom fitted with a walk-in shower. Two further bedrooms on the first level each include built-in storage and private en-suites, with the principal bedroom featuring a twin bathroom with a freestanding bath and underfloor heating.

The second floor opens to light-filled living and dining zones, framed by full-height glass that captures views of plane trees through sheer linens and sliding doors. Deep-toned cabinetry contrasts with lighter oak and slimline stainless steel across the island, while integrated Fisher & Paykel appliances, including a French-door fridge and induction cooktop, are complemented by a butler's pantry fitted with a Vintec wine fridge and microwave convection oven.

Above, a vast rooftop terrace offers sweeping treetop views and irrigated garden beds, creating a breathtaking outdoor refuge that extends from the upper-level living room. Engineered oak floors, reverse-cycle heating/cooling, laundry, ample storage, double glazing, Faucet Strommen tapware, and solar power complete a peerless residence, all within a prized lifestyle local, moments from Central Park, East Malvern village, Darling Station, elite schools, and Hedgeley Dene Gardens.

title: 16 Ruabon Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Exclusively Situated Edwardian Elegance
description_plain:
Tastefully transformed into a modern and light-filled urban oasis, this renovated and extended three bedroom, two bathroom Edwardian residence is ready to be enjoyed in a premier position within easy walking distance of Hawksburn Village, Beatty Avenue and Toorak Railway Station. Set within a lush established garden, the home creates soothing outdoor connections to suit families or even downsizers looking for a taste of sanctuary in a vibrant Toorak setting.

At its heart, an unashamedly contemporary open-plan rear with semi-attached dining reveals a vogue black-and-white colour palette, complemented by attractive timber-toned flooring, plantation shutters and leafy outlooks, all anchored by a gourmet Bosch-equipped kitchen that is arranged around a welcoming waterfall-edged island of stone. Stepping outdoors seamlessly to paved entertaining and an elevated garden backdrop, the home suits an alfresco lifestyle, while additional highlights include a fully-tiled and skylit bathroom with heated towel rail all themed to the primary bedroom’s ensuite, plus there are built-in robes in all bedrooms and a security alarm, as well as split system heating / air conditioning and a double carport with additional driveway parking.

Walk easily to the refined café culture, fine dining and boutique shopping of Hawksburn and Toorak Village as well as Beatty Avenuen, plus you have Orrong Park, Toorak Park, multiple trams and city bound trains from Hawksburn and Toorak Railway Stations.

title: 87a Mathoura Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Classic Elegance on the Village Fringe
description_plain:
Secured behind a gated entry in one of Toorak's most prized village settings, this elegant contemporary residence delivers an exceptional lock-up-and-leave lifestyle with independent street presence and extraordinary proportions.

Interiors celebrate a northern aspect, with a grand entry hall revealing high ceilings, porcelain-tiled flooring, and brilliant sunlight from a central courtyard that flows into the formal sitting room. Double doors provide fantastic separation to the impeccably appointed kitchen with sleek stone benchtops, integrated refrigeration, and premium appliances, including twin ovens with slide-and-hide doors and an induction cooktop. The sunken living and dining areas with gas fireplace opens seamlessly to a private garden courtyard, enveloped in lush mature greenery beneath a sheltering pergola for year-round alfresco living.

Generous in scale, the upper level features three spacious bedrooms with fitted robes, including an expansive main suite with a walk-in dressing room and deluxe en-suite, accompanied by a central second bathroom. Moments from Toorak station, celebrated cafes and restaurants, trams, Orrong Park and elite schools, it includes intercom, reverse-cycle air conditioning, powder room, substantial laundry, irrigation, ample storage, lift space provision and an internally accessed tandem garage with additional off-street parking for one.

title: 1932-1934 Malvern Road, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Period Charm in Stunning Garden Surrounds - 793sqm approx.
description_plain:
Peacefully set well back from the street behind a high hedged garden, this utterly captivating c1920’s solid brick residence’s evocative single level dimensions deliver immediate family appeal with scope to extend in the future into the deep private garden. 
A majestic triple arched verandah introduces interior spaces replete with ornate ceilings, leadlight windows and timber floors. The wide entrance hall flows through to a generous living room and gracious dining room both with decorative mantelpieces. The gourmet kitchen appointed with stainless steel appliances and stone benches is adjacent to a study nook and the inviting family room opening to the deep private leafy garden with a large, paved terrace. The three double bedrooms are complemented by a stylish bathroom with bath, plus a separate powder room., 
Desirably situated close to East Malvern station, Waverley Rd shops and cafes, great schools, Waverley Oval and Gardiner’s Creek bike path, it includes ducted heating and cooling, 2nd toilet, laundry, storage and garage. Land size: 793sqm approx.

title: 204 Waverley Road, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Flawless Style & Convenience
description_plain:
Showcasing elite quality, flawless designer style and sublime luxury, this recently renovated stunning 4 bedroom/3-ensuite contemporary residence delivers the perfect low maintenance lifestyle for the busy family. Privately positioned behind a high fenced garden and secure frontage, in the tightly held Ardrie Park precinct.
Filled with natural light and intelligently designed for modern family life, the floorplan provides excellent zoning and flexibility. A welcoming entrance hall leads to a separate sitting/media room with custom window seat, while a fitted home office opens to a private side deck - ideal for working from home.

At the heart of the home, the expansive light-filled living and dining domain is anchored by a gas pebble fireplace and a sleek gourmet kitchen appointed with stone benchtops, two Bosch ovens and a generous walk-in pantry and abundant storage rarely seen in low maintenance homes. Stacking glass sliders seamlessly connect the interior to a private easy-care garden and substantial entertaining deck. Bi-fold servery windows from the kitchen enhance indoor-outdoor flow, creating a superb setting for year-round alfresco dining.

The ground-floor master suite is a true retreat, complete with walk-in robe and a luxurious ensuite finished with striking herringbone stone tiles, rain shower and premium fittings. Upstairs, a second main bedroom with robes and designer ensuite provides excellent flexibility for guests or teenagers, complemented by two additional robed bedrooms sharing a stylish ensuite and a separate rumpus retreat. 
Importantly, the home offers three distinct living zones, allowing families to come together while still enjoying their own space. All needed amenities are downstairs making it perfect also for downsizers who want to accommodate guests or intergenerational living. 

Additional features include reverse cycle heating and cooling, powder room, full laundry, automatic gate and internally accessed garage.
Waverley Road’s cafés and shops, trams, Caulfield Station, quality primary and secondary schools and the Caulfield campus of Monash University, are on conveniently on your doorstep within short walking distance. This is an opportunity to secure contemporary comfort, privacy and lifestyle convenience in one of Malvern East’s most desirable pockets.

title: 10/712 Orrong Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: ‘Myrnong Close’: Tranquil, Light-filled Icon
description_plain:
Evoking a tranquil sense of sanctuary within easy walking distance of Toorak Village, this spacious two bedroom plus study, first floor apartment is set to the very rear of the tightly held ‘Myrnong Close’ building – a mid-century icon crafted by the renowned Frederick Romberg of ‘Grounds, Roy and Romberg’. North-facing, gloriously light-filled and overlooking lush communal gardens, the apartment is a proud Toorak icon with a lifestyle to match.

With windows everywhere and natural light filling its every corner, a generously sized dining / living room is backed by an impressive galley-style kitchen with plenty of built-in storage, while the primary bedroom has been completed with an entire wall of built-in robes. Character filled and with polished hardwood under foot, the apartment also features a brilliant study with a built-in desk and storage, plus there is a pristine, fully-tiled bathroom and the year-round comfort of split system heating / air conditioning.

This is a blue-chip location poised for you to enjoy all the trappings of a Toorak lifestyle within easy walking distance of the refined café culture, thriving bars, restaurants and boutique shopping of the village, where the Number 8 tram can take you to Chapel Street and the CBD, whilst you also have all the endless parkland and bike paths of the Yarra River just at the other end of the road.

title: 13 Newry Street, Prahran, 3181
heading: Prahran’s Secret Garden
description_plain:
Magnificently set within an exceptionally deep and extremely rare 637m² (approx.) landscaped northwest garden, and with no heritage overlay, this captivating freestanding period residence offers outstanding potential for future extension or redevelopment (STCA), while exceeding all expectations in terms of sun-drenched family space, contemporary style and environmental sustainability.

Behind the classic exterior, a contemporary sensibility takes over through interior dimensions that embrace garden outlooks at every turn. Timber floors flow through the entrance gallery to a superbly proportioned dining room and gourmet granite kitchen, both opening to a private north-facing deck beneath a large pergola. Spectacular in scale, the living room extends out to a full-width undercover deck and the impressively deep and beautifully landscaped west-facing garden, offering plenty of room to extend or add a pool.

A spacious bedroom with robes and en suite, a second double bedroom with robes and a stylish bathroom/laundry are on the ground level. In its own private sanctuary upstairs, the stunning main bedroom with ample robes and an en suite flows out to a private west-facing balcony enjoying wonderful views over the garden and city skyline.

In a tranquil location close to Hawksburn Village, Chapel Street’s shops and restaurants, Windsor station, trams, schools and Victoria Gardens, it includes hydronic heating, RC/air-conditioning, double glazing, irrigation, water tanks, solar panels and battery, ample storage and two off-street car parks, offering both lifestyle and future development opportunities in one exceptional package.

title: 1/2015 Malvern Road, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Private. Polished. Perfect.
description_plain:
Tucked privately behind a lush, manicured hedge and set well back from the street, this impressive executive residence offers a beautifully light-filled environment designed for effortless, low-maintenance living. Showcasing refined contemporary style, it delivers a superb standard of quality throughout.
Warm timber floors and abundant natural light enhance the spacious living room, which flows seamlessly to an inviting dining area and a gourmet kitchen complete with stone benchtops and Bosch appliances. Sliding glass doors reveal a secluded, north-facing courtyard framed by leafy greenery, perfect for private outdoor enjoyment. The flexible floorplan includes a ground-floor guest bedroom or home office with its own ensuite, while upstairs features three generous bedrooms with built-in robes, serviced by a luxurious bathroom with a bath.
Ideally positioned close to Darling Station, the cafés and shops of Darling Village, quality schools, Hedgeley Dene, Gardiners Creek and Chadstone Shopping Centre, this home is further enhanced by features such as reverse-cycle air conditioning, a European laundry, ample storage, an automatic gate, garage, and additional off-street parking.

title: 177 Williams Road, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Village Victorian Grandeur with Secure Garage
description_plain:
Expressions of Interest Close Monday 10th March at 3pm

Embracing a glorious garden setting at the heart of Hawksburn Village, this magnificent balconied Victorian residence’s elegant refinement and grandeur form the perfect backdrop for an esteemed city-edge lifestyle.

High-walled privacy and a tessellated tile verandah introduce a traditional arched hallway, leading past a gracious sitting room into a sublime formal dining room; both featuring tall ceilings, timber floors, and ornate beauty. Breathtaking northern sunlight flows from the lightwell courtyard into the formal dining room, a connection reflected in the relaxed living and dining areas beyond, and is complemented by a central, well-appointed kitchen with quality appliances and solid timber cabinetry. Bi-fold doors lead out to a private paved garden bordered with mature greenery, perfectly shaded for al-fresco dining and offering easy access to the sizable double remote garage conveniently accessed from Howitt Street behind.

Upstairs, the beautifully proportioned main bedroom with an as-new ensuite, OFP and a full-width north-facing balcony headlines the spacious accommodations, with double glazing ensuring absolute removal from the street below. Two additional oversized bedrooms follow to the rear, including a larger rear bedroom with city views, as well as a beautiful, marble-detailed bathroom with twin pedestal basins and a claw-foot bath.

In a prestigious pocket, mere moments from an array of cafes and restaurants, Malvern Road trams, Hawksburn Station, premium dining precincts, and elite schooling options, it includes a powder room, hydronic heating, garden irrigation, ample storage, and an attic with a pull-down ladder.

title: 79 Brunel Street, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Blue-Chip Lifestyle, Exceptional Landholding
description_plain:
Enjoy the very best of a prized, tree-lined neighbourhood, just moments from parklands, cafés, transport and leading schools. Perfectly positioned within walking distance of Hedgeley Dene, Central Park, Burke Road’s vibrant shopping and dining strip, and Darling Station, this address delivers an enviable family lifestyle of convenience and connection.

Set on a deep, private north-facing allotment of approximately 631sqm with the rare advantage of dual street frontages, this immaculately maintained brick residence offers immediate comfort alongside outstanding future potential.

Designed to capture abundant northern light, the spacious floorplan features expansive living and dining domains with an open fireplace, a modern central kitchen, and a generous family room with timber floors. Both living zones extend effortlessly to a large, sail-shaded deck overlooking the deep, private garden with rear access to Carlyle Way—creating the perfect setting for relaxed family living and entertaining.

Accommodation is both flexible and functional, with a ground-floor double bedroom, while upstairs hosts the main bedroom with built-in robes and ensuite, two additional bedrooms with built-in robes, and a bright central bathroom with a separate WC.

Additional features include ducted heating, reverse-cycle air conditioning, powder room, full laundry and off-street parking for two cars.
Alternatively, and further enhancing the appeal, the property comes with approved permit for a new luxury residence.

title: 18 Ethel Street, Malvern, 3144
heading: Executive Excellence on an Exceptional Scale
description_plain:
Showcasing refined contemporary elegance, elite quality and exceptional proportions, this luxurious 4-bedroom/4-ensuite executive residence is a serene sanctuary for relaxed living, working from home and lavish indoor-outdoor entertaining.
An oversized pivot door reveals interior spaces distinguished by wide recycled Jarrah floors and high ceilings. The entrance hall flows down to a graciously proportioned formal sitting and dining room with a gas log fire and backdrop of lush greenery. Equally generous in scale, the open plan living and dining room is served by a gourmet kitchen appointed with beautiful marble benches, Miele/Bosch appliances, walk in pantry and study nook. Full height glass sliders extend living options out to a very large stone paved elevated terrace with a striking water feature. Surrounded by Gardenias and Magnolias, it’s a private oasis for al fresco dining. A home office or guest bedroom with a stunning en suite and desk opens to the picturesque, landscaped forecourt with a water feature. Broad skylit stairs lead up to the expansive main bedroom with walk in robe and lavish en suite with a deluxe spa bath, two additional robed bedrooms with stylish en suites and a retreat. On the lower level, a 3 ½ car garage with an abundance of storage is accessed via a wide right of way.
Peacefully located close to Menzies Reserve while also offering close proximity to Tooronga station, Malvern and Tooronga Villages, trams, elite schools and freeway access, it includes an alarm, video intercom, ducted heating/cooling, ceiling fans, ducted vacuum, powder-room, laundry, irrigation, EV charger and under house storage. Land size: 348sqm approx.

title: 64 Surrey Road, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Victorian Charm with Vibrant Style
description_plain:
Idyllically positioned directly opposite Surrey Park, this classic solid brick Victorian residence has been brilliantly renovated with innovative designer flair and bespoke finishes to create an irresistibly alluring inner urban lifestyle sanctuary.
Designer colours, soaring 3.5m ceilings, industrial style hung doors and limed timber floors are introduced in the wide arched entrance hall, complete with skylight and two gorgeous double bedrooms with robes, original heritage roses, fireplaces and a stunning bathroom featuring a freestanding bath and heated floors. A sensational living and dining domain with a gas log fire and gourmet kitchen appointed with Calacatta marble waterfall benches, Miele oven and integrated Liebherr fridge/freezer is bathed in year-round light from a northern courtyard. Full width black steel framed glass doors open the living to a beautiful leafy, landscaped northeast garden oasis with built in seating and roller door access to a rear lane allowing possible off-street parking. 
In a pocket prized for its enviable proximity to Toorak Rd and Chapel St shops and restaurants, Prahran Market, Hawksburn Village, Hawksburn station, trams and schools, it includes an alarm, RC/air-conditioning throughout, Euro-laundry, ample storage and garden shed.

title: 2/6 Lalbert Crescent, Prahran, 3181
heading: Art Deco Masterpiece with Private Terrazzo
description_plain:
A rare top-floor Art Deco residence of impressive scale and timeless elegance, this exclusive c1924 home offers privacy, character, and exceptional outdoor living. A private, expansive terrazzo-style courtyard with soft vine coverage delivers a peaceful and secluded outdoor escape.
Positioned on the Penthouse level, the home enjoys picturesque leafy outlooks from every room. A tiled entrance introduces a grand living room with an open fireplace, seamlessly connecting to an equally impressive dining room. Both spaces feature original burnished timber sliding doors and classic Art Deco details that define its enduring elegance.
The magnificent main bedroom features original robes, accompanied by a second bedroom with robes. A sun-filled north-facing home office or third bedroom adds further flexibility, while a stylish bathroom enhances the home’s refined character.
The separate kitchen offers generous storage and practical functionality without compromising on design. Descending the rear stairs leads to an extraordinary black-and-white tiled courtyard with a substantial vine-covered pergola, ideal for year-round alfresco dining and entertaining.
Superbly positioned close to High Street shops, cafés, and trams, as well as Toorak Station, leading schools, and Orrong Park, this distinctive residence also includes premium appointments such as security intercom, zoned reverse-cycle air conditioning, remote blinds, Euro-laundry, auto gate, garage, and an additional car space.

title: 13 Forster Avenue, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Big Family Living on a Brilliant North-West Block
description_plain: A rare combination of character, scale, and flexibility, this extended c1930s residence is set on a generous north-west allotment of approx 736sqm (18.29m frontage approx) and delivers outstanding family accommodation with a private poolside backdrop. Beyond its timeless façade, the home unfolds to reveal multiple living zones, 5 possibly 6 well-proportioned bedrooms, and excellent separation for growing families. At the heart of the home, light-filled living and dining areas connect effortlessly to a sun-drenched outdoor domain complete with in-ground swimming pool, paved entertaining terrace, and lush tropical surrounds. Adding exceptional versatility is a fully self-contained studio with sauna and independent access, ideal for guests, teenagers, multigenerational living, or a dedicated work-from-home retreat. Superbly located close to Darling Train Station, Central Park and Koornang Road shops, St Mary’s and Lloyd Street primary schools, Korowa, Sacre Coeur, Caulfield Grammar, Monash Uni, Ardrie Park and the Gardiners Creek Trail, this is a compelling opportunity to secure space, lifestyle, and flexibility in a prized family-friendly setting.

title: 1413 Dandenong Road, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Prime Chadstone Position Offering Exceptional Redevelopment Potential
description_plain:
Positioned just metres from Chadstone Shopping Centre, this renovated five-bedroom Californian Bungalow offers exceptional lifestyle comfort and outstanding future potential within the Residential Growth Zone (RGZ).

Set on 650sqm (approx.), the home features bright open-plan living, a granite-topped kitchen, a separate rumpus with wet bar, and a north-facing outdoor entertaining area with an outdoor kitchen. The main bedroom includes both BIR and WIR plus an ensuite, supported by a family bathroom with spa and multiple additional toilets. Heating/cooling, security shutters, alarm system, video intercom, tandem garage and gated parking add further convenience.

In the RGZ, this property sits within a high-amenity location earmarked for increased housing density—offering significant development prospects thanks to its proximity to major transport, schools, parklands and Chadstone’s retail and employment hub.

A rare opportunity combining immediate family comfort with strong long-term growth and redevelopment potential (STCA).

title: Garden Residence, 1042 Malvern Road, Armadale, 3143
heading: The Garden Residence - Elegance Grounded in Nature
description_plain:
Inspect By Private Appointment 

Nestled within The Albany Armadale at 1042 Malvern Road, the Garden Residence offers an exquisite balance of refined design and natural serenity in one of Melbourne’s most prestigious addresses. Designed by Christopher Doyle Architects, this exceptional ground-floor home reflects the timeless grace of Armadale, blending classic architectural details with contemporary sophistication to create a residence of enduring beauty and comfort.
Spanning 169sqm of elegantly appointed interiors and opening to a single expansive 97sqm private courtyard, this three-bedroom, three-bathroom residence is defined by its generosity of space and effortless connection to the outdoors. Light-filled living and dining areas flow seamlessly through full-height glass doors to the landscaped courtyard — a tranquil oasis ideal for entertaining or peaceful retreat.
The main suite exudes calm and refinement, featuring plush wool carpets, a bespoke walk-in robe and a luxurious ensuite with a double vanity and premium finishes. Two additional bedrooms, each with their own bathrooms, provide flexibility and privacy for family or guests, enhancing the home’s sense of comfort and ease.
Every element within The Garden Residence has been thoughtfully curated to balance luxury and liveability, from the palette of natural materials to the timeless craftsmanship evident throughout. With two secure car spaces and a rare connection to nature in the heart of Armadale, this residence perfectly embodies the quiet sophistication and enduring allure of The Albany.
Positioned moments from the boutiques and cafés of High Street, The Albany places you at the centre of Armadale’s most refined lifestyle offerings. Stroll to renowned dining destinations, charming village shopping, and leafy parks, with excellent transport links and some of Melbourne’s most prestigious schools all within easy reach. Here, convenience and elegance unite in one of the city’s most sought-after neighbourhoods.

title: 90 St Georges Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Prestigious Holding with Dual Frontages and City Views
description_plain:
Inspect by Private Appointment – Please contact Jack Nicol directly

An outstanding proposition on one of Melbourne's most prestigious boulevards, this long-held European-inspired residence spans approximately 1,020sqm with two street frontages and a prized northern rear aspect. Surrounded by mature formal landscaping and high-fenced privacy and security, it offers the opportunity to enjoy the property immediately while presenting scope to shape its next chapter in an esteemed riverside position.

Classical European influences introduce refined interiors, commencing with a dramatic circular foyer that leads to a formal dining room suited to a 24-seat setting, an elegant sitting room and a lounge with an open fireplace, and a stone-finished kitchen with direct garden access for catered occasions. A peaceful living area to the rear unfolds onto the elevated terrace, which flows down to the north-facing heated pool, framed by established greenery. The upper level includes four bedrooms and three bathrooms, two of which feature private en-suites. The main suite extends to a dressing area and a substantial northern terrace overlooking the Yarra River and the city skyline. A ground-floor study with storage offers flexibility as a fifth bedroom, supported by a powder room, lower-level laundry, cellar, home automation, CCTV, video intercom, extensive storage and an outdoor gardeners' WC.

Secure vehicle entry via Orrong Road allows for parking for four or more cars, complemented by a double-remote garage and a double carport behind automated gates. Options to renovate, enhance, or rebuild (STCA) strengthen the long-term appeal, only moments from Toorak Village, leading independent schools, the CBD, and major arterials.

title: 66 River Street, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Unbeatable Style and Lifestyle
description_plain:
In the beating heart of South Yarra with its enormous lifestyle opportunities, this sensational contemporary multi-level residence’s stylish, sun-drenched and versatile spaces provide endless options for lock up and leave living, rooftop entertaining and running a business from home.
Designed around a central lightwell, every room is flooded with natural light. Timber floors flow through the generous open plan living and dining room featuring a gourmet Smeg kitchen and north-facing balcony. On the top level, a second sun-drenched living area with a wet bar opens out to a large rooftop deck providing an evocative inner urban outdoor entertaining venue. The two double bedrooms with built in robes and en suites enjoy their own separate levels while on the ground floor a spacious home office or third bedroom opens to the lightwell. 
In an activity centre zone offering flexible use (STCA), it’s just metres to Chapel St and Toorak Rd shops and restaurants, the Vogue Centre, Como Cinemas, South Yarra station, trams and the Yarra River. Additional features include alarm, video intercom, ducted heating/cooling, powder-room, laundry, storage and internally accessed garage for 2 cars.

title: 34 Alice Street, Malvern, 3144
heading: Prime Renovation Opportunity or New Home Site
description_plain:
Set on a desirable parcel in a quiet, tree-lined street, this property presents an outstanding opportunity to renovate, rebuild or create your dream home (STCA). Offering excellent orientation with northern light and a flexible existing layout, the foundations are here for those looking to add significant value or start again with a bespoke design.

The current two-storey residence provides multiple living zones, a central courtyard and established garden surrounds, along with off-street parking via a double carport. While the home is in need of renovation, it offers scope for those with vision to transform the existing footprint or utilise the land to construct a luxury new residence tailored to modern family living (STCA).

Positioned for lifestyle and convenience, the property enjoys easy access to some of Melbourne’s finest shopping precincts including Glenferrie Road, Malvern Road, Toorak Road and High Street, along with an array of cafés, restaurants and boutiques. Close to leading schools including Malvern Central Primary, parklands such as Malvern Gardens, and excellent public transport options, this is a rare chance to secure a premium site in a tightly held location.

Land size: 462sqm approx.

title: Sub-Penthouse, 1287 High Street, Malvern, 3144
heading: Timeless architecture meets elevated design
description_plain:
With a prized North-West aspect, poised above the tree-lined streets of Malvern, this exclusive three-bedroom, three-bathroom sub-penthouse defines refined modern living. Framed by sweeping views and bathed in natural light, every space has been masterfully composed to balance timeless elegance with contemporary sophistication.

Drawing inspiration from Malvern’s leafy charm, the building’s powerful linear form and earthy material palette- bronze-toned cladding, glass, and raw concrete- express a deep connection to place. Inside, bespoke finishes and curated details reflect a design philosophy grounded in beauty, comfort, and longevity.

The expansive open-plan living area seamlessly connects to a private terrace, ideal for effortless entertaining or quiet reflection. A gourmet kitchen, fitted with Miele appliances, Signorino stone, and an integrated wine fridge, celebrates both craftsmanship and functionality. Each bedroom offers serene privacy, while the master suite delivers hotel-style indulgence with its luxurious ensuite and custom joinery.
Every element of the sub-penthouse experience has been carefully considered-enhanced by sustainable innovation and premium inclusions throughout.

Features include:
• 7.5 Star NatHERS Rating
• Miele Appliances
• Wine Fridge
• Integrated Fridge / Freezer
• Keyless Entry Throughout
• Secure Parcel Lockers
• Double Glazed Windows
• Lights by Sphera
• Stone by Signorino
• Flooring by George Feathers
• Fireplace & Custom TV Joinery
• Two Secure Car Parks

Malvern Gardens is more than a residence- it is a statement of refined living, where architectural excellence and natural beauty exist in perfect harmony.

title: 13 Tooronga Road, Malvern East, 3145
heading: Architectural Sanctuary of Contemporary Luxury
description_plain:
Architect-designed and crafted to exacting standards, this resolutely stylish residence is a rare contemporary statement in a blue-chip pocket. Set on an expansive 766sqm (approx.) north-east allotment, this exquisitely appointed family haven sits beyond secure walls, auto gates and refined landscaping, revealing an exceptionally striking façade and a dramatic double-height entry that introduces sculptural glass lighting, a broad central hall and engineered timber floors. Grand, light-filled scale immediately sets a luxurious tone.
A secluded formal living room frames the picturesque front garden, reflecting the home’s tranquil ambience. The flexible ground-floor accommodation includes a graciously proportioned bedroom suite with bespoke walk-through robes and rain shower marble ensuite, plus a study/home office or sixth bedroom to suit multi-generational living. Family practicality is elevated with an oversized double garage, utility entry, a superb laundry appointed with abundant storage and external access, and a sophisticated powder room.
At the rear, a soaring living and dining domain amplifies volume and light, defined by a showpiece entertainer’s kitchen with a dramatic Vermont White Dolomite waterfall island/breakfast bar and coordinating feature wall. Custom cabinetry, top-of-the-range Miele appliances - including dual ovens - integrated refrigeration, walk-in pantry, and a Vintec wine fridge complete the space, while cohesive detailing sees Tundra Grey marble carried through the adjoining second kitchen, laundry and all ensuites. The rear dining and living zone features a refined linear gas fireplace with floating marble shelving, creating an environment that is both elegant and effortless to live in.
Full-height sliders connect to a private, split-level garden oasis: tree-lined lawns, a pool and an expansive, partially covered terrace with built-in barbecue and outdoor kitchen designed for year-round entertaining.
Via a floating staircase, the upper level opens to the sumptuous main suit offering a beautifully tailored dressing room, vanity station and a hotel-style ensuite with freestanding tub, twin vanities, rain shower and plentiful storage. Three further bedrooms are each generously scaled with robes and their own ensuites, with a marble-clad wet bar and an elegant retreat space beyond offering an additional living zone for quiet relaxation.
Every detail within this meticulous residence speaks to enduring quality and quiet prestige. Ducted heating/cooling, intercom and CCTV enhance comfort and security throughout.
Moments from esteemed Malvern Primary, leading private schools, Monash University, parklands, Malvern and Caulfield stations, and premier shopping and dining, this tranquil haven is a singular offering of luxury, privacy and rarity.

title: 56 Moore Street, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Architectural Brilliance Bathed in Light
description_plain:
Set against an exquisite garden backdrop, this Nicholas Day–designed residence delivers refined contemporary living with exceptional family flexibility. Peacefully positioned within a quiet streetscape, it opens to soaring ceilings, generous proportions and two distinct living zones—each spilling onto private alfresco terraces bathed in soft morning and afternoon light.

At the heart of the home, a striking marble kitchen anchors daily life and entertaining with Miele appliances, an integrated dishwasher and a welcoming breakfast bar perfect for relaxed meals and conversation.

Upstairs, three spacious bedrooms with built-in robes offer superb accommodation, headlined by a luxurious main suite with marble ensuite, walk-in robe and a balcony capturing sweeping city views. A fourth bedroom or home office with a built-in study nook adds versatility, while a sleek central bathroom enhances everyday ease.

Premium details include a guest powder room, slate floors, Euro laundry, skylights, intercom and split-system heating and cooling throughout. Secure garaging with remote access opens directly to Argo Park, making it ideal for professionals, young families and pet lovers seeking green open space right at the doorstep.

Enviably positioned moments from Toorak Road and Commercial Road trams, South Yarra Station, Prahran Market and the boutiques and cafés of Chapel Street, it promises an effortlessly connected inner-urban lifestyle.

title: 28 Hill Street, Toorak, 3142
heading: An Elite Collaboration in Modern Design
description_plain:
Inspect By Private Appointment at the Advertised Times

Designed by the acclaimed Nicholas Day with landscaping by Jack Merlo, this brand-new contemporary residence exemplifies architectural elegance and refinement. Its linear, geometric façade, four lift-connected levels and elevated city skyline vistas form a standard of living reserved for Melbourne’s most exceptional homes.

Marble-tiled floors and 3.1m-high ceilings introduce generously scaled interiors, showcasing the impeccable attention to detail from the very first step. The palatial ground floor features a refined office with a luxe semi-en suite, formal living and dining rooms with verdant outlooks, and expansive living and meals areas designed to integrate with the gardens. Calacatta marble creates a consistent visual thread through the living and kitchen domains, wrapping the gas fireplace and the benchtops. Premium Gaggenau appliances and integrated Liebherr refrigeration feature throughout, including within the secondary butler’s kitchen, while an outdoor Wolf BBQ supports the capacity for entertaining al-fresco, overlooking landscaped gardens and a heated self-cleaning pool.

The first floor houses four lavish bedroom suites, each appointed to an elite standard. The main suite offers a private lounge, a designer dressing room, an accessory closet and a marble bathroom with an Apaiser bath as its centrepiece. The upper level functions independently, with a generous living and kitchen area opening to a terrace that frames panoramic city views. A guest bedroom with an en suite completes the floor, offering flexibility for extended family or guests, with lift access connecting every level. Below, the basement unfolds as a leisure wing featuring a climate-controlled wine cellar, a large gym, powder room, home theatre and a six-car garage with secure entry.

Comprehensively appointed, it also features an internal lift, comprehensive security systems, two powder rooms, multi-zone reverse-cycle heating/cooling, underfloor heating in bathrooms, automated irrigation, motorised blinds and video intercom entry. Within a tree-lined locale, close to Toorak Village, city-bound transport, leading schools, and the Yarra River trails, this remarkable home represents the highest calibre of living in one of Melbourne’s most connected and renowned settings.

title: 114 Millswyn Street, South Yarra, 3141
heading: A Botanical Backdrop to Design Brilliance
description_plain:
Inspect By Private Appointment

The result of a visionary transformation by architect Oliver Du Puy with landscaping by the acclaimed Kate Seddon, this 1889 Victorian residence, as featured in Vogue Living, EST Living and Architectural Digest, captures a captivating dialogue between period architecture and contemporary design. Fronting the iconic Maples warehouse mews with its own street frontage and title, it enjoys complete independence while sharing access to the shared landscaped gardens and pool beyond, forming part of one of the Domain Precinct's most exclusive residential communities.

A masterful use of proportion, colour, and light reflects the architect's international inspiration. The design draws through a discreet vestibule garden entry into a west-facing formal living room with a working fireplace, extending through French doors to a lush courtyard. The kitchen and main living areas extend outward to the verdant garden surrounds, set beneath 3.5m-high exposed beam ceilings, and finished with Calacatta Vagli marble, Moroccan Zellige tiles and premium appliances, including a La Cornue range cooker. Every detail celebrates quality, juxtaposing it with imperfections through the marks of hand-finished timber and the changing light that filters through steel-framed doors, which unfold for indoor-outdoor living.

Upstairs, the scale continues within the three bedrooms, each featuring built-in robes and unfolding through double doors. The two bathrooms are softened in custom hues and enriched by marble and bronze details. The sanctuary-like main en-suite features motorised blinds for added privacy and adjoins the large, king-sized suite with a walk-in robe and a stunning eastern garden outlook.

Designer lighting forms part of the home's energy, featuring a curated mix of contemporary pieces from New York studios and rare European antiques. Craftscale solid oak joinery and imported English hydronic heating panels continue the bespoke brilliance, with additional features including double glazing, reverse-cycle air conditioning, underfloor heating in the bathrooms, and parking for two. Moments from Melbourne Grammar, the Tan, Gilsons, Baker Bleu, Anzac Station, and the nearby arts precinct, the location defines the art of inner-city living. A landmark heritage home revived to exceed modern-day expectations, timeless in presence, refined in detail, and truly unforgettable.

title: 2 Towers Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Landmark Estate in Toorak’s Finest Pocket
description_plain:
Inspect Strictly By Private Appointment 

Encompassing close to an acre of mature, park-like gardens with a pool and north-south tennis court, this Mediterranean Revival estate spans approximately 3,322sqm in one of Toorak’s most revered enclaves. Private and architecturally imposing, it carries a quiet international pedigree and a standing rarely available in the city’s residential landscape, set beyond automated gates and high garden walls on the corner of Towers and Orrong Road.

Designed by Catt Architects, the residence is introduced by a sweeping circular driveway that opens onto impeccably landscaped Paul Bangay designed gardens, where manicured hedges, cascading wisteria, and perfectly striped lawns elegantly frame the home. Garaging for four vehicles, ample guest parking and a highly adaptable interior support both private living and grand-scale entertaining. Inside, six-metre ceilings rise above a magnificent foyer, introducing an executive office, an elegant lounge, and a formal dining room, each oriented toward tranquil garden or pool aspects. 

The Gaggenau-appointed Arabescato Marble kitchen is designed for everyday living as well as entertainment on a grand scale, seamlessly flowing into informal living and dining spaces that encourage connection and ease. Upstairs, five en-suite bedrooms offer far-reaching outlooks, including a lavish main suite and an additional sixth suite, currently arranged as a custom designer wardrobe. An independently accessed dual-level dwelling offers exceptional flexibility for guests, complete with a fully appointed marble bar, a living room, and an upper-level bedroom featuring an en-suite bathroom and access to both indoor and outdoor spaces. Multiple living zones extend across the home, including a vast multi-purpose games room, complemented outdoors by a gymnasium, solar-heated lap pool, and a series of inviting dining terraces for year-round enjoyment.

An extraordinary estate with a level of luxury seldom seen on the open market, finished with a comprehensive sweep of high-end appointments, including state-of-the-art home automation, technology and security, a chef’s commercial kitchen for catering, a basement with a temperature-controlled cellar, lift provisions, and self-contained staff quarters.

title: 29 Lansell Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: Toorak Estate with Grand Family Living
description_plain:
Inspect By Private Appointment at the Advertised Times

Commanding a rare landholding of 2,787sqm (approx) among Toorak’s most prestigious addresses, this magnificent 1928 English Revival residence unites distinctive period architecture with expansive family amenity, surrounded by park-like gardens, a pool and a north-south tennis court. Distinguished by its steep gables, red-brick driveway entry, and solid brick façade, the imposing foyer, rich in era-defining detail, leads into a sequence of elegant formal rooms, including dining, living, and a refined study, each enjoying verdant views and exceptional proportions.

Transformed for contemporary living and large-scale entertaining, the informal living and dining domain extends to a chef-inspired kitchen appointed with a Wolf cooktop and ovens, induction cooking, and integrated refrigeration. A bespoke hardwood table inset within the quartzite island, complements the hosting appeal, with bi-fold doors connecting the family areas to the elevated game rooms and indoor-outdoor dining room, set above the pool and tennis court. Beyond, manicured gardens with brick pathways lead to a self-contained guest house, playground and relaxed outdoor zones, all privately enclosed by mature greenery.

A grand staircase ascends to six bathrooms and six generously sized bedrooms, all with their own en-suites, including a luxurious main bedroom suite with a lavish Arabescato marble bathroom and fitted dressing room. Modern infrastructure underscores its capacity for family living, featuring an internal lift, powder room, gym, sauna, and kitchenette that cater to casual al-fresco dining. Prestigiously positioned within moments of Heyington Station, trams and Melbourne’s leading schools and within moments of Kooyong Lawn Tennis Club and M1 access, it offers peerless amenity in one of Toorak’s most revered settings. Comprehensively appointed with ducted reverse-cycle air conditioning, gas fireplace, comprehensive security, garden irrigation, abundant storage, garages/workshops and sheds, plus ample vehicle accommodation beyond dual automated gates, with two double garages plus broad undercover parking for six.

title: The Penthouse/79-83 Wattletree Road, Armadale, 3143
heading: Move In June 2026 – A One-of-a-Kind Penthouse Residence at Society Armadale
description_plain:
Currently under construction with Krongold Construction - Melbourne’s premier builder renowned for delivering the city’s most prestigious homes - this penthouse is a statement of scale, luxury, and exclusivity rarely seen in apartment living. 

Spanning an extraordinary approx. 1,168sqm, this residence is more akin to a palatial home than a penthouse. Designed for both grand entertaining and private family living, it features a home cinema, wine cellar, office, sauna, multiple living zones, and retreat spaces that allow family members to gather or enjoy their own space in style. 

Accessed via its own private lift and foyer, the penthouse also boasts a private gym and rooftop pool with uninterrupted views across Melbourne, stretching from the bay to the city skyline. 

With only 17 residences in the building, exclusivity is assured. Residents will enjoy the services of a dedicated concierge and thoughtful amenities including a private dining room, car wash, dog wash and the convenience of the WeWumbo building management app. 

Perfectly positioned next to Malvern Central and just a short stroll to Glenferrie Road and High Street shopping, with effortless freeway connections, this is a residence that defines modern prestige. 

A truly unique home unlike anything else on the market.

title: 4/113 Osborne Street, South Yarra, 3141
heading: Award-Winning Architectural Statement with No Owners Corporation
description_plain:
A serene fusion of architecture, light, and landscape, this newly completed three-bedroom, two-and-a-half-bathroom residence delivers an inspired vision of contemporary urban living. Conceived by award-winning architects and landscape designers, every element has been thoughtfully composed to achieve a sense of calm sophistication—where refined form meets lush, natural surrounds in perfect balance.

Bathed in light and surrounded by sculpted greenery, the home’s minimalist aesthetic celebrates space, texture, and timeless design. The Bosch-appointed kitchen, beautifully integrated with marble finishes and concealed joinery, anchors the main living zone with quiet confidence. Expansive glass sliders open to a bluestone-paved courtyard framed by Star Jasmine and a tranquil reflection pool—an effortlessly elegant setting for relaxation or entertaining.

A dramatic double-height atrium floods the interior with natural light, connecting both levels with a striking architectural rhythm. Upstairs, three generously proportioned bedrooms are complemented by an open study that continues the home’s serene design language. The primary suite enjoys a private terrace overlooking South Yarra Siding Reserve, a skylit walk-in robe, and a luxurious natural stone ensuite.

Attention to detail is evident throughout, with premium inclusions such as full double glazing, French Oak flooring, bespoke joinery, designer lighting, reverse-cycle climate control, and an ethanol fireplace. Secure garaging and an additional gated off-street space further enhance the home’s functionality and privacy.

Positioned within South Yarra’s vibrant heart, the residence offers unmatched access to Toorak Road and Chapel Street’s celebrated dining, shopping, and café culture. The Como Centre, Prahran Market, trams, and South Yarra Station are moments away—delivering an impeccable lifestyle of sophistication and connection.

title: 22 Douglas Street, Toorak, 3142
heading: Uncompromising Luxury by the Village
description_plain:
Inspect By Private Appointment at the Advertised Times
Selling in Conjunction with Jamie Mi (Kay & Burton) - 0450 125 355

Defined by the peerless quality and meticulous eye of Royale Constructions and Christopher Doyle, this exquisite residence is a bespoke expression of inspired design and unwavering luxury, discreetly positioned steps from Toorak Village.

Symmetry carries from the distinctive brick façade through light-filled interiors and onward to the arched loggia framing the poolside garden. Each detail has been purposefully curated, from solid joinery and European marble to signature open fireplaces, imported French chandeliers, and the fine bronze trim that delineates marble and wide oak flooring.

Ceilings rising from 3.2 to 3.6m invite an abundance of natural light into formal reception rooms and expansive living areas, where arched motifs are mirrored in the formal rooms' unique coved ceilings and steel-framed glass overlooking the pool. The sense of scale extends to the marble kitchen, fitted with Gaggenau appliances, Miele integrated dishwashers, Liebherr refrigeration, and a fully appointed butler's pantry. A plumbed barbecue rests beneath rich timber ceilings outdoors, enhanced by overhead heating and ceiling fans for year-round entertaining beside the solar-heated pool and striking landscaped garden. Below, ornate coffered ceilings crown the grand entertainment room, featuring a marble bar with Vintec wine storage, a Miele dishwasher, and an adjoining temperature-controlled cellar with cedar joinery and herringbone brick detailing.

The palatial northern main suite offers total seclusion, with a designer-worthy dressing room and a luxurious marble-detailed bathroom. Three additional bedroom suites with walk-in robes are positioned to the rear, complemented by a lower-level guest suite that broadens the scope for family needs. A lavish home theatre, executive office, Tylo cedar sauna, and gym with an adjoining bathroom further enrich the light-filled layout, with an seven-car basement garage beyond automated gates, offering 3-phase EV provisions.

title: 701/58 Porter Street, Prahran, 3181
heading: Unprecedented Urban Elegance with Stunning Views
description_plain:
Breathtaking in scale and appointed without even a hint of compromise, this brand new three bedroom, two bathroom residence is defined by one of urban Melbourne’s finest and most sundrenched terraces where panoramic views span from the waters of Port Phillip Bay to the towers of the CBD to make your home entertaining an unforgettable experience. Carefully curated by Cera Stribley Architects in collaboration with Outline, the home presents a staggering 244 square metres of indoor-outdoor living space in an authentically Prahran, parkside position tucked away between Chapel Street, Prahran Market and Greville Street.
Tastefully synthesizing a suite of Miele appliances with marble working surfaces and a cocktail bar as well as an integrated refrigerator and rich lime Oak flooring, the kitchen / meals / living is luxuriously elegant as it flows effortlessly through three sets of double doors to the terrace where the city views and the sunsets are simply stunning. Meanwhile, each of the bedrooms is impressively scaled with huge amounts of custom robe storage and a primary bedroom ensuite that is fully tiled and particularly vogue.
Fully secure within this carbon neutral, all electric building with solar power, EV charging capability and side-by-side basement parking for three vehicles, this remarkable home also enjoys access to a breath-taking rooftop terrace to take your home entertaining to another level. Further highlights include a storage unit in the basement, a built-in BBQ on the terrace and a study with two workstations, plus there is central and zoned reverse cycle heating and air conditioning.
Evoking a soothing sense of parkside sanctuary in the heart of Prahran, this is a thriving location that allows you to wander to an endless selection of cafes and bars, whilst some of Melbourne’s finest restaurants are at your doorstep, Prahran market is just metres away and multiple transport options can take you to the CBD or the rest of Melbourne in no time.

title: 7A Monomeath Avenue, Toorak, 3142
heading: Architectural Brilliance and Lift-Connected Luxury in Blue-Chip Kooyong
description_plain:
Architecturally conceived by acclaimed designer Nicholas Day, this exceptional contemporary town residence delivers a sophisticated family domain of impressive scale, refined elegance and lift-connected convenience, all in a blue-chip Kooyong setting.
Beyond its striking façade, high ceilings and oak parquetry floors introduce beautifully proportioned interiors, where bespoke finishes and considered detailing create an atmosphere of understated luxury. Zoned living areas provide enviable flexibility for families, with generous accommodation thoughtfully separated to ensure both connection and retreat, ideal for those with teenage children.
At the heart of the home, expansive open-plan living and dining zones are bathed in abundant northern light, underscored by a welcoming gas log fireplace and seamless indoor-outdoor integration. The gourmet kitchen is a statement in both form and function, appointed with a sublime Calacatta marble island bench, premium appliances and a fully equipped butler’s pantry, designed for effortless entertaining at scale.
Four impressively proportioned bedrooms include a palatial main suite offering a lavish dressing room and designer en suite complete with a freestanding bath, creating a private sanctuary of hotel-like indulgence. An executive study provides a refined work-from-home environment, complemented by an additional fitted study area with extensive storage.
The lower level enhances the home’s versatility, featuring a dedicated theatre room and a yoga and gym domain, while an oversized double garage with sink and internal lift access ensures practicality without compromise.
Positioned just metres from Kooyong Village’s boutique shops and cafés, the station, trams, prestigious schools, parklands, bike trails and freeway access, this is a rare opportunity to secure a luxurious, low-maintenance residence in one of Melbourne’s most tightly held and prestigious precincts.

title: 7 Yarradale Road, Toorak, 3142
heading: English Elegance with a Designer Perspective
description_plain:
Designed by prominent architect Robert Hamilton and recently transformed by the esteemed team at Colab Design Studio, this English-style residence delivers a rare architectural lineage in one of Melbourne’s most distinguished riverside settings. Settled within established and magnificent Paul Bangay-designed gardens and relishing all-day sun from a northern rear aspect, it preserves its bygone elegance, while naturally adapting to contemporary family needs.

Beyond the towering liquid ambers and hydrangeas of the front garden, a grand foyer introduces the substantial layout with double brick proportions and leadlight beauty, revealing formal and informal zones and superb indoor-outdoor flow to the north-facing rear. Natural light sweeps through both levels, drawing attention to the refined updates that elevate each space. Formal living and dining rooms are enriched by a striking bay window and original fireplace, while the second living area, comprising wall-to-wall storage, opens to a European-style terrace beneath a canopy of wisteria and climbing ivy. Calacatta marble finishes define the kitchen, including hand-cut herringbone tiling, a hidden cocktail bar with a Vintec wine fridge, and an Italian Bertazzoni stove, all of which reflect the subtle luxury. French doors extend the casual living room to a self-cleaning, heated pool and integrated BBQ terrace, ideal for both private calm and weekend entertaining amongst a stunning landscape of seasonal colour. 

The lavish main bedroom is a serene sanctuary upstairs, featuring a luxurious marble en suite and extensive custom robes. Two additional generously sized en-suite bedrooms follow on the same floor, each sharing the stylish marble bathroom, with a hotel-inspired bath and separate WC, with a ground-floor study offering flexibility as a fourth bedroom. In a tightly held location, moments from elite schools, the Yarra River trail, the Monash Freeway, and Toorak Village, this exquisite private home is comprehensively appointed with a monitored alarm, dual-zoned ducted heating/cooling, a powder room, laundry, abundant storage, and secure off-street parking for two, accessed via automated gates. 
Land size: 499 sqm approx.

title: 390 Glenferrie Road, Malvern, 3144
heading: Modern Family Sanctuary With Historic Charm on Chesterfield Avenue Corner
description_plain:
Occupying a magnificent 1,047sqm (approx.) corner allotment in one of Malvern’s most prestigious pockets, 390 Glenferrie Road is a superbly renovated solid brick residence circa 1907, seamlessly fusing timeless architectural grandeur with contemporary family luxury.
Behind its elegant period façade, this substantial five-bedroom, three-bathroom home showcases exquisite original features including soaring decorative ceilings, ornate open fireplaces, leadlight windows and rich period detailing, creating a warm and sophisticated atmosphere throughout.

Recently renovated to the highest standard, with sublime finishing touches by interior stylist Nancy Tripodi of My Atelier, the home has been thoughtfully designed to support modern family living while honouring its historic charm. A flexible and generously proportioned floorplan reveals a series of refined formal and informal living and dining zones, all flooded with natural light and beautifully suited to both everyday living and grand-scale entertaining. At the heart of the home, the contemporary kitchen is superbly appointed with quality European appliances, marble benchtops and generous storage, providing a natural gathering place for family and guests alike.

The second level, complete with lift access, opens to two wide veranda terraces capturing sweeping city skyline views and beyond — ideal for morning coffee or evening relaxation. Five generous bedrooms and a separate study are complemented by three stylish bathrooms, while the master suite offers a private sanctuary with walk-in robe, ensuite, and a light-filled reading nook.

Set within beautifully landscaped gardens with ample entertaining space and off-street parking, the home’s elevated corner position delivers both presence and privacy, being set back behind high fences with primary access via automated gates on Chesterfield Avenue. Perfectly located with Sir Robert Menzies Reserve at the end of the street, and within walking distance to Kooyong Village, Kooyong Station, multiple tram routes, Kooyong Tennis Club and Melbourne’s finest schools, this is an exceptional family address.

Offering a rare combination of heritage elegance, modern comfort, substantial proportions and unrivalled lifestyle amenity, this is a once-in-a-generation opportunity to secure a landmark family home in one of Melbourne’s most tightly held and prestigious neighbourhoods.
